# Project Catalog (153 projects)

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 172,037
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
The self-improving AI agent that grows with you — persistent memory, auto-generated skills, 14 platforms, 6 execution backends

Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent, multi-platform task execution and self-improvement. It utilizes a closed learning loop to create and refine skills from experience while building long-term user models through memory nudges and session searches. The agent integrates with 14 messaging platforms and supports six execution backends, including serverless environments like Modal and SSH-connected clusters. Users can swap between hundreds of LLM providers and models via a unified CLI or gateway process without changing code.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience.
- Cross-platform support for Telegram, Discord, Slack, and terminal interfaces.
- Six execution backends including Docker, SSH, and serverless persistence.

---

## farion1231/cc-switch

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/farion1231/cc-switch
GitHub: https://github.com/farion1231/cc-switch
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 84,139
Cross-platform all-in-one manager for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent

CC Switch is a cross-platform manager designed to centralize the administration of multiple AI coding tools. It is built using the Tauri 2 framework to provide a unified interface for managing various CLI-based agents. The tool integrates support for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent. This allows users to switch between different AI coding environments within a single application across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Highlights:
- Supports Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent
- Cross-platform compatibility for Windows, macOS, and Linux
- Developed using the Tauri 2 framework

---

## mem0ai/mem0

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mem0ai/mem0
GitHub: https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 57,028
Universal memory layer for AI Agents — official Hermes Agent memory provider, available as managed cloud or Apache-2.0 self-hosted OSS

Mem0 is a universal memory layer designed to provide AI agents and assistants with long-term context and personalization. It utilizes a multi-signal retrieval system that combines semantic search, BM25 keyword matching, and entity linking to store and recall user preferences and session history. The system features a single-pass extraction algorithm that accumulates memories without overwriting, enabling temporal reasoning for past, present, and future events. It is available as a self-hosted Apache-2.0 library, a Docker-based server, or a managed cloud service.

Highlights:
- Multi-level memory retention for users, sessions, and autonomous agents
- Hybrid retrieval fusing semantic, keyword, and entity-based signals
- Temporal reasoning for ranking memories by relevant dates and states

---

## nexu-io/open-design

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nexu-io/open-design
GitHub: https://github.com/nexu-io/open-design
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 55,092
Local-first open-source design/prototyping system with coding-agent CLI support including Hermes Agent

Open Design is an open-source, local-first design and prototyping system that serves as an alternative to Claude Design. It functions by wiring existing coding-agent CLIs on the user's PATH into a skill-driven workflow via a local daemon and web app. This architecture allows users to maintain a Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) approach while leveraging a library of composable skills and design systems to generate design artifacts. The system supports web deployment to Vercel and provides a sandboxed iframe for live previews.

Highlights:
- Auto-detects 16 coding-agent CLIs including Hermes Agent
- Includes 137 composable skills and 150 design systems
- Supports HTML, PDF, PPTX, ZIP, and Markdown exports

---

## colbymchenry/codegraph

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/colbymchenry/codegraph
GitHub: https://github.com/colbymchenry/codegraph
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 32,388
Local pre-indexed code knowledge graph for Hermes Agent and other coding agents to reduce token usage and tool calls

CodeGraph is a local pre-indexed code knowledge graph designed to reduce token consumption and tool call frequency for coding agents. It replaces expensive file scanning and grep operations by providing agents with instant access to symbol relationships, call graphs, and code structure. This approach allows agents to answer architectural questions more efficiently, often reducing tool calls by an average of 57%. It integrates with various agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini.

Highlights:
- Provides local semantic intelligence via pre-indexed knowledge graphs
- Reduces average tool calls by 57% and costs by 18%
- Supports Hermes Agent, Cursor, Claude Code, and other coding agents

---

## iOfficeAI/AionUi

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/iOfficeAI/AionUi
GitHub: https://github.com/iOfficeAI/AionUi
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 27,097
Local open-source cowork app for OpenClaw, Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and other agent CLIs

AionUi is a cross-platform desktop application that provides a unified graphical interface for local AI agents to perform autonomous tasks. It integrates a built-in agent engine with full file system access, web searching, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) tool support to automate workflows like document generation and web browsing. The platform also auto-detects and manages external CLI agents such as Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, allowing users to coordinate multiple agents simultaneously. Beyond manual interaction, it supports 24/7 scheduled automation via Cron and remote access through messaging platforms like Telegram and WeChat.

Highlights:
- Unified GUI for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and 13+ CLI agents
- Built-in engine for generating editable PPT, Word, and Excel files
- Supports 24/7 unattended automation using Cron and remote messaging access

---

## volcengine/OpenViking

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/volcengine/OpenViking
GitHub: https://github.com/volcengine/OpenViking
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 24,874
Open-source context database for AI agents — official Hermes Agent memory provider using viking:// URIs and tiered L0/L1/L2 loading; 91% token reduction and 43-49% task-completion gain over baseline on LoCoMo10. AGPL-3.0.

OpenViking is an open-source context database designed to solve the problem of fragmented memory and inefficient context management in AI agents. It replaces traditional flat vector storage with a filesystem paradigm to unify the organization of memories, resources, and skills. The system utilizes tiered loading and recursive directory retrieval to reduce token consumption and improve retrieval precision. It integrates with agents like Hermes and OpenClaw to enhance long-context QA accuracy and task success rates.

Highlights:
- Filesystem paradigm for unified memory and resource management
- L0/L1/L2 tiered context loading to reduce token costs
- Visualized retrieval trajectories for observable context debugging

---

## supermemoryai/supermemory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/supermemoryai/supermemory
GitHub: https://github.com/supermemoryai/supermemory
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 22,744
Memory engine and API — official Hermes Agent memory provider with sub-300ms recall sustained at 100B+ tokens/month, custom vector graph engine, context fencing, and byte-level dedupe with 100% prompt-cache discount.

Supermemory is a memory and context engine that provides a persistent information layer for AI agents and applications. It functions by automatically extracting facts from conversations, maintaining dynamic user profiles, and managing knowledge updates or contradictions through a custom vector graph engine. The system integrates RAG, file processing, and real-time connectors into a single API to deliver relevant context with sub-300ms recall. It supports various clients via an MCP server and offers dedicated plugins for tools like Hermes, Claude Code, and Cursor.

Highlights:
- Automated fact extraction and user profile maintenance from conversations.
- Sub-300ms recall with byte-level deduplication and prompt-cache discounts.
- Native connectors for Google Drive, Notion, GitHub, and Gmail.

---

## garrytan/gbrain

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/garrytan/gbrain
GitHub: https://github.com/garrytan/gbrain
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 19,639
Garry's Opinionated OpenClaw/Hermes Agent Brain

GBrain is a memory and context layer for AI agents that transforms raw page search into synthesized answers. It utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph to create typed edges between entities without LLM calls, combined with a synthesis layer for cited prose and gap analysis. The system can be deployed as a personal knowledge base or a scoped company brain with permission-based data access. It integrates with agents like OpenClaw and Hermes and exposes over 30 tools via the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

Highlights:
- Synthesizes cited answers with explicit gap analysis of missing information
- Self-wiring knowledge graph for complex entity relationship traversal
- MCP support for integration with Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf

---

## vectorize-io/hindsight

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/vectorize-io/hindsight
GitHub: https://github.com/vectorize-io/hindsight
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 15,068
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Agent memory that learns — long-term retain/recall/reflect workflows

Hindsight is an agent memory system designed to enable long-term learning and information retention for autonomous AI agents. It utilizes a workflow of retain, recall, and reflect operations to move beyond simple conversation history and build persistent mental models. The system can be integrated via an LLM wrapper for automatic memory management or through direct SDK calls for granular control. It supports multi-user isolation through metadata filtering and is compatible with major LLM providers including OpenAI, Anthropic, and Ollama.

Highlights:
- Implements retain, recall, and reflect workflows for long-term learning
- Two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory storage and retrieval
- Supports PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database for enterprise storage

---

## mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
GitHub: https://github.com/mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 11,718
754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, ATLAS, D3FEND & NIST AI RMF

Anthropic Cybersecurity Skills is an open-source library of 754 structured skills designed to provide AI agents with the domain-specific knowledge of a senior security analyst. Each skill follows the agentskills.io standard, utilizing YAML frontmatter and structured Markdown to define step-by-step execution workflows and decision-making logic. The library maps every skill across five major frameworks, including MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, and the NIST AI RMF. It is compatible with over 26 platforms such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, and Hermes Agent for use cases in threat hunting, digital forensics, and cloud security.

Highlights:
- 754 structured skills across 26 security domains for AI agents.
- Unified mapping to MITRE ATT&CK, ATLAS, D3FEND, and NIST frameworks.
- Follows agentskills.io standard for sub-second discovery and execution.

## nesquena/hermes-webui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nesquena/hermes-webui
GitHub: https://github.com/nesquena/hermes-webui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 9,131
Hermes WebUI: The best way to use Hermes Agent from the web or from your phone!

Hermes WebUI is a lightweight, self-hosted browser interface for the Hermes Agent that provides a graphical alternative to the CLI. It is built using Python and vanilla JavaScript without the need for a build step or framework. The interface features a three-panel layout for session management, chatting, and file browsing, offering full parity with the agent's terminal capabilities. Users can securely access their existing agent and models via an SSH tunnel with minimal configuration.

Highlights:
- Three-panel layout with session, chat, and file browsing
- Full feature parity with the Hermes CLI experience
- No-build setup using only Python and vanilla JavaScript

Desktop companion app for installing, configuring, and chatting with Hermes Agent

Hermes Desktop is a native GUI companion for the Hermes Agent that simplifies the installation and management of the self-improving AI assistant. The application automates dependency resolution and environment setup, allowing users to interact with the agent through a streaming chat interface rather than a CLI. It provides centralized control over 14 toolsets, 16 messaging gateways, and complex features like cron-based task scheduling and memory management. Users can run the agent on a local backend or connect to remote API servers while managing multiple isolated profiles and model providers.

Highlights:
- Automated guided installation for local or remote Hermes Agent backends
- Management of 16 messaging gateways including Telegram, Discord, and Slack
- Integrated tools for memory editing, persona configuration, and task scheduling

Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive dashboard for managing Hermes Agent through a responsive web interface. It provides a centralized control plane for real-time AI chat, session management, and multi-platform channel configuration across services like Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. The system features a self-built SQLite session database, usage analytics for cost tracking, and a multi-profile architecture for isolated agent environments. Users can also manage scheduled cron jobs, browse remote files, and access an integrated web terminal for direct system interaction.

Highlights:
- Unified configuration for eight messaging platforms including Slack and WhatsApp

Stars: 5,056
Self-hosted AI agent orchestration — dispatch tasks, run multi-agent workflows, monitor spend

Mission Control is a self-hosted dashboard designed to orchestrate and monitor fleets of AI agents. It utilizes a local-first architecture powered by SQLite and Next.js to coordinate multi-agent workflows, dispatch tasks, and track operational costs without external database dependencies. The platform features built-in quality gates for task approval, real-time telemetry via WebSockets, and a security framework that includes secret detection and trust scoring. It integrates with various agent frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph, and provides a bridge for CLI tools such as Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Self-hosted orchestration using SQLite with zero external dependencies
- Multi-gateway support for CrewAI, LangGraph, AutoGen, and Claude SDK
- Built-in Aegis review system for human-in-the-loop task sign-off

## outsourc-e/hermes-workspace

Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 5,018
Native web workspace — chat, terminal, memory browser, skills manager, and inspector panel

Hermes Workspace is a native web-based command center designed to provide a unified graphical interface for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It integrates real-time chat, a cross-platform terminal, and a file browser with specialized tools for managing agent memory, skills, and Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers. The platform features a 'Swarm Mode' that uses persistent tmux-backed workers to orchestrate multiple autonomous agents across role-based dispatch lanes. Users can monitor agent state, track costs, and manage complex missions through a centralized dashboard and Kanban task board.

Highlights:
- Unified interface for chat, terminal, memory editing, and skill management.
- Swarm Mode for orchestrating multiple persistent, role-based autonomous agents.
- Zero-fork compatibility with vanilla Hermes Agent and dashboard installations.

## campfirein/byterover-cli

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/campfirein/byterover-cli
GitHub: https://github.com/campfirein/byterover-cli
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 4,802
Memory as a git repo (formerly Cipher) — official Hermes Agent memory provider with 5-tier retrieval (4 tiers non-LLM, sub-100ms), brv vc commit/branch/merge versioning over plain markdown context tree. Elastic License 2.0.

ByteRover CLI is a persistent memory provider for AI coding agents that structures project knowledge into a version-controlled context tree. It utilizes a 5-tier retrieval system and a git-like workflow—including branching, committing, and merging—to manage markdown-based memory. The tool integrates with over 20 LLM providers and 22 AI agents, offering a REPL interface and a web dashboard for curating codebase information. It supports the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and provides cloud synchronization for team collaboration and cross-device access.

Highlights:
- Git-like version control for context including branching and merging
- Sub-100ms 5-tier retrieval system for long-term conversational memory
- Built-in agent tools for file operations and code execution

## plastic-labs/honcho

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plastic-labs/honcho
GitHub: https://github.com/plastic-labs/honcho
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 4,455
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Memory library for building stateful agents — the upstream library that the elkimek/honcho-self-hosted Hermes wrapper builds on.

Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to build stateful agents that maintain context across users, groups, and projects over time. It functions by storing conversation logs and events, then using background reasoning processes to extract conclusions and update persistent peer representations. Developers can query these representations for natural-language insights or retrieve prompt-ready context for injection into LLM calls. The system supports hybrid search and is available as a managed service or a self-hosted FastAPI server with SDKs for Python and TypeScript.

Highlights:
- Extracts reasoning-based conclusions from conversations instead of simple vector matching
- Maintains evolving representations for users, agents, and specific project entities
- Provides prompt-ready context compatible with OpenAI and Anthropic message formats

## alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book
GitHub: https://github.com/alchaincyf/hermes-agent-orange-book
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 3,911
Hermes Agent practical guide — Orange Book series (Chinese language)

The Hermes Agent Orange Book is a practical guide designed to help users master the Hermes Agent open-source AI framework. It explains the framework's technical architecture, including its three-layer memory system, self-improving learning loop, and automatic skill evolution. The guide provides 17 chapters of content covering installation, customization, and real-world applications such as development automation and knowledge assistants.

Highlights:
- Detailed guides on memory systems and learning loops
- Step-by-step setup and multi-platform customization instructions
- Practical scenarios for dev automation and multi-agent systems

---

## NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-agent-self-evolution
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 3,674
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Evolutionary self-improvement using DSPy + GEPA — optimizes skills, prompts, and code

Hermes Agent Self-Evolution is an automated optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes Agent without requiring GPU training. It utilizes DSPy and Genetic-Pareto Prompt Evolution (GEPA) to analyze execution traces and mutate skill files, tool descriptions, and system prompts based on reflective feedback. The system validates candidate variants through constraint gates, including size limits and full test suite passes, before proposing improvements via pull requests. It supports evaluation using both synthetic data and real session history from tools like Claude Code and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Optimizes agent skills and prompts using DSPy and GEPA engines
- Operates via API calls without requiring local GPU resources
- Enforces strict guardrails including size limits and 100% test pass rates

---

## 0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/0xNyk/awesome-hermes-agent
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 3,548
Curated list of awesome skills, tools, integrations, and resources for Hermes Agent

This project is a curated directory of resources designed to expand the capabilities and workflows of the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It organizes community-contributed skills, tools, integrations, and documentation into categorized lists for easier discovery. Users can find everything from deployment utilities and messaging bridges to specialized domain applications and multi-agent orchestration tools. The repository also includes maturity tags to help developers distinguish between stable production tools and experimental proofs of concept.

Highlights:
- Curated list of community skills and plugins
- Categorized resources for deployment and integrations
- Maturity tags for assessing tool stability

---

## junhoyeo/tokscale

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/junhoyeo/tokscale
GitHub: https://github.com/junhoyeo/tokscale
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 3,319
CLI tool for tracking token usage from Claude Code, OpenClaw, Hermes, Codex, Gemini, and more

Tokscale is a high-performance CLI tool and visualization dashboard designed to track token consumption and costs across various AI coding agents. It functions by scanning local databases, session logs, and configuration directories to aggregate usage data from multiple providers into a unified interface. The tool features a native Rust TUI for real-time monitoring and supports a web-based frontend with 3D contribution graphs. It enables developers to audit their spending and usage patterns across a wide range of platforms including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and GitHub Copilot.

Highlights:
- Tracks token usage and costs across multiple AI coding agents
- Features a native Rust TUI and 3D visualization dashboard
- Supports integrations for Hermes, Claude, Gemini, and Cursor

---

## agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires
GitHub: https://github.com/agent-of-empires/agent-of-empires
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 2,451
TUI and web control surface for managing multiple coding agents including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex, Gemini, and others

Agent of Empires is a session manager for AI coding agents on Linux and macOS that simplifies the management of multiple parallel agent workflows. It utilizes tmux to ensure sessions persist across terminal crashes or reboots, providing a TUI and web dashboard for monitoring agent status. The tool supports isolated environments via Docker sandboxing and git worktrees to allow agents to operate on different codebase branches simultaneously. It integrates with a wide range of agents, including Hermes, Claude Code, and Gemini, and offers remote access via Tailscale Funnel or Cloudflare Tunnel.

Highlights:
- TUI and web dashboards for multi-agent session monitoring
- Docker, Podman, and Apple Containers for agent sandboxing
- Parallel execution across git worktrees and multi-repo workspaces

---

## Agents365-ai/drawio-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/Agents365-ai/drawio-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,953
Generate draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions

This project is a diagramming skill that converts natural-language descriptions into professional .drawio XML files and high-resolution image exports. It utilizes the native draw.io desktop CLI to render diagrams and features a self-correction mechanism that analyzes its own output to fix layout issues like overlapping labels or stacked edges. The tool supports six specialized presets including UML, ERD, and machine learning architectures while allowing users to capture and reuse custom visual styles from existing files. It integrates with various AI agents such as Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes through the Agent Skills format.

Highlights:
- Generates .drawio XML and exports to PNG, SVG, PDF, or JPG
- Self-checks and auto-fixes layout issues using visual feedback loops
- Six diagram presets including Architecture, UML, and Deep Learning

---

## dodo-reach/hermes-desktop

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
GitHub: https://github.com/dodo-reach/hermes-desktop
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 1,675
A native Mac workspace for Hermes: real SSH, real terminal, real session data.

Hermes Desktop is a native macOS application that provides a focused workbench for managing Hermes Agent hosts. It establishes a direct connection over SSH to interact with remote sessions, files, and workflows without requiring a browser or additional daemon. The app maintains the remote host as the single source of truth, allowing users to manage multiple agent profiles, edit skills, and monitor usage. It also features an embedded terminal and local workflow presets to streamline agent interactions on Apple Silicon and Intel Macs.

Highlights:
- Connects directly to Hermes hosts via native SSH and aliases
- Manages remote sessions, Kanban boards, and cron jobs natively
- Supports multi-agent workflows through selectable Hermes profiles

---

## conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
GitHub: https://github.com/conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,600
Audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns

This project is a portable writing skill designed to audit and rewrite text to eliminate detectable AI patterns and "AI-isms." It utilizes a structured two-pass detection system that cross-references content against a 109-entry word replacement table and 46 distinct pattern categories. Users can choose between full rewrites, a detect-only mode for auditing, or an edit-in-place mode for minimal targeted changes. The tool integrates with various agent frameworks including Hermes, Claude Code, and OpenClaw, supporting custom voice profiles to maintain specific brand tones.

Highlights:
- Two-pass audit system catches patterns that survive initial edits
- Tiered 109-entry word replacement table for precise vocabulary control
- Supports three modes: Rewrite, Detect-only, and Edit-in-place

---

## AMAP-ML/SkillClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AMAP-ML/SkillClaw
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,534
Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver

SkillClaw is a skill management framework that enables AI agents to evolve their capabilities through real-world interactions. It functions by running a background evolution loop that automatically deduplicates, improves, and merges skills across different sessions and devices. The system creates a unified skill library that synchronizes experience between multiple agents and users, ensuring that improvements made by one instance are available to all others. It natively integrates with the Hermes Agent ecosystem and supports various other platforms including OpenClaw, QwenPaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs.

Highlights:
- Automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops
- Unifies skill libraries across multiple agents, devices, and user teams
- Natively integrates with Hermes, OpenClaw, and OpenAI-compatible APIs

---

## NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/hermes-paperclip-adapter
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,428
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Run Hermes as a managed employee in Paperclip company systems

The hermes-paperclip-adapter is a bridge that allows Hermes Agent to function as a managed employee within the Paperclip company ecosystem. It works by wrapping the Hermes CLI to parse raw terminal output into structured transcript entries, enabling Paperclip to render interactive tool cards and status icons. The adapter manages session persistence across heartbeats, allowing agents to maintain context while responding to task assignments or issue comments. It also synchronizes skills between local directories and the Paperclip interface while reclassifying system logs to ensure a clean user experience.

Highlights:
- Integrates 30+ native tools and 80+ skills into Paperclip
- Parses raw CLI output into structured markdown and tool cards
- Supports eight inference providers including Anthropic, OpenAI, and OpenRouter

---

## NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/Hermes-Function-Calling
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,367
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Function calling examples and training data for Hermes LLM models

This repository provides the implementation and training framework for enabling function calling and structured JSON output in Hermes 2 Pro models. It utilizes a recursive loop to generate, execute, and interpret tool calls based on provided schemas, supporting complex multi-turn interactions. Users can define custom tools via Python functions or enforce specific data structures using Pydantic models. The project includes specialized scripts for financial data retrieval and demonstrates compatibility with the ChatML prompt format.

Highlights:
- Enables recursive function calling and structured JSON mode inference
- Supports custom tool definition via Python and Pydantic schemas
- Implements ChatML prompt formatting for OpenAI-compatible multi-turn dialogue

---

## numtide/llm-agents.nix

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/numtide/llm-agents.nix
GitHub: https://github.com/numtide/llm-agents.nix
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 1,320
Nix packages for AI coding agents including Hermes

llm-agents.nix provides a curated collection of Nix packages designed to streamline the installation and management of AI coding agents and development tools. It functions as a centralized repository that automatically updates daily to ensure users have access to the latest versions of various CLI-based agents. The project enables developers to easily deploy tools like multi-agent orchestrators, terminal-based coding assistants, and unified API proxies. This ecosystem supports a wide range of providers including OpenAI, Claude, Gemini, and xAI.

Highlights:
- Daily automatic updates for all included packages
- Supports diverse AI coding agents and orchestrators
- Provides unified proxy APIs for multiple model providers

---

## jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP
GitHub: https://github.com/jau123/MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 1,302
Supports GPT Image 2, Nanobanana & ComfyUI, with a 1,400+ prompt library, carefully crafted hooks and a multi-task orchestration system

MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that integrates professional image and video generation into AI coding environments. It connects to nine leading models, including Midjourney and Flux, through three backend modes: MeiGen cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs, or local ComfyUI instances. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is designed for use in tools like Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and Cursor to facilitate design tasks like logo creation and product rendering.

Highlights:
- Supports 9 image and video models including local ComfyUI integration
- Includes 1,446 curated prompt templates for style-aware generation
- Orchestrates parallel batch generation via sub-agents for complex design tasks

---

## NousResearch/atropos

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/atropos
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/atropos
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,240
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
RL training environments framework for tool-calling models

Atropos is an environment microservice framework designed for asynchronous reinforcement learning with large language models. It operates through a trajectory API that facilitates data exchange between environment services and external trainers or inference engines. The system supports diverse interactive settings including tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal tasks to guide models toward optimal performance. Notable applications include training models for complex multi-turn interactions and specialized financial prediction tasks.

Highlights:
- Microservice framework for asynchronous LLM reinforcement learning environments
- Trajectory API for standardized data collection and batch processing
- Supports tool-calling, code execution, and multimodal environment types

---

## greyhaven-ai/autocontext

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
GitHub: https://github.com/greyhaven-ai/autocontext
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 1,161
Recursive self-improving context harness — helps agents succeed on complex tasks

Autocontext is a recursive self-improving harness designed to help AI agents succeed on complex tasks through iterative evaluation. It operates by running a multi-role loop—including competitors, analysts, and coaches—that tests strategies against real-world scenarios and discards ineffective approaches. Successful outcomes are distilled into structured playbooks, datasets, and traces that future agent iterations inherit as persistent knowledge. The system integrates with various providers like Anthropic and OpenAI, and offers specific CLI and MCP support for Hermes Agent and Claude Code.

Highlights:
- Iterative self-improvement loop using five specialized agent roles
- Generates structured playbooks and traces for persistent knowledge transfer
- Native integration with Hermes Agent via CLI-first skill exports

---

## wondelai/skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/wondelai/skills
GitHub: https://github.com/wondelai/skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 1,142
Cross-platform skills library for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents

Wondel.ai Skills is a cross-platform library of agent capabilities designed for Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible environments. It provides pre-configured skill collections based on established industry frameworks, such as Jobs to Be Done, Clean Code, and Lean Startup methodology. Users can install these skills via the Claude Code marketplace or a dedicated CLI to equip AI agents with specialized knowledge in software architecture, UX design, and product strategy. The project bridges the gap between raw LLM capabilities and structured professional methodologies through modular, installable plugins.

Highlights:
- Compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compatible agents
- Modular installation via marketplace or npx skills CLI
- Frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy

---

## NousResearch/autonovel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/NousResearch/autonovel
GitHub: https://github.com/NousResearch/autonovel
Category: Core & Official
Stars: 1,025
Official: Yes (maintained by Nous Research)
Autonomous novel-writing pipeline generating 100k+ word manuscripts

Autonovel is an autonomous agent pipeline designed to transform a seed concept into a complete, publishable novel. It utilizes a multi-phase workflow that includes world-building, sequential drafting, and an adversarial revision loop involving dual-persona LLM reviewers. The system integrates mechanical slop detection and voice fingerprinting to ensure prose quality and narrative consistency across tens of thousands of words. Beyond text generation, the project automates typesetting in LaTeX, cover art creation via fal.ai, and multi-voice audiobook production using ElevenLabs.

Highlights:
- Autonomous four-phase pipeline from world-building to final export
- Adversarial revision loop with mechanical and LLM-based evaluation
- Automated typesetting, cover illustration, and multi-voice audiobook generation

---

## liaohch3/claude-tap

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/liaohch3/claude-tap
GitHub: https://github.com/liaohch3/claude-tap
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 979
Local trace viewer for inspecting coding-agent API traffic from Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Cursor, OpenCode, and others

Claude-tap is a local proxy and trace viewer designed to inspect API traffic from AI coding agents like Hermes Agent and Claude Code. It functions by running CLI agents through a local proxy to capture and display real-time data including system prompts, tool schemas, and token usage. Users can debug agent behavior by comparing request diffs and inspecting reconstructed streaming responses directly in a web-based viewer. The tool prioritizes privacy by keeping traces local and automatically redacting common authentication headers.

Highlights:
- Inspects prompts, tool calls, and streaming responses in real-time
- Supports Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Cursor, and Gemini CLI
- Generates self-contained HTML artifacts for sharing and archiving traces

---

## ksimback/hermes-ecosystem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
GitHub: https://github.com/ksimback/hermes-ecosystem
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 907
Hermes Atlas — the community map of every tool, skill, and integration for Hermes Agent by Nous Research

Hermes Atlas is a community-curated directory and ecosystem map for the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It aggregates over 80 quality-filtered repositories, including skills, plugins, and deployment templates, using a vanilla JavaScript frontend and Vercel serverless functions. The platform features a RAG-powered chatbot that answers technical questions grounded in 27 research files using hybrid retrieval and OpenRouter LLMs. Users can search and filter the ecosystem while viewing live GitHub star counts and growth trends cached via Redis.

Highlights:
- Directory of 80+ security-reviewed Hermes Agent tools and integrations
- RAG chatbot providing grounded answers from 27 research files
- Live GitHub star tracking with Redis-cached sparklines and trends

---

## CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room
GitHub: https://github.com/CryptoDmitry/hermes-agent-control-room
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 844
Control Room-first template for managing Hermes agents from one VPS agent to specialist teams and orchestrated workflows

The Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based management framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents from a single VPS. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured environment for documenting agent registries, runbooks, and security protocols. Users can scale from a single agent to a multi-agent system using a task bus pattern for delegation between an optional orchestrator and specialized agents. The repository includes bundled skills for VPS provisioning, automated backups, and security auditing to ensure consistent operations across an agentic team.

Highlights:
- Provides a sidecar control plane for agent documentation and governance.
- Scales from single agents to orchestrated multi-agent specialist teams.
- Includes task bus patterns for structured orchestrator-to-specialist delegation.

---

## AxDSan/mnemosyne

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AxDSan/mnemosyne
GitHub: https://github.com/AxDSan/mnemosyne
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 723
The Zero-Dependency, Sub-Millisecond AI Memory System for Hermes Agents

Mnemosyne is a universal memory layer that provides AI agents with a local, persistent storage system for long-term context. It utilizes a Bilevel Episodic-Associative Memory (BEAM) architecture backed by a single SQLite database for hybrid vector and full-text search. The system supports various integration methods including an MCP server, a Python SDK, and native plugins for tools like Cursor and OpenWebUI. This approach allows agents to maintain private, offline memory with minimal resource overhead.

Highlights:
- Local-first storage using SQLite with sqlite-vec and FTS5
- BEAM architecture featuring working, episodic, and triple-store memory
- Broad compatibility via MCP, Python SDK, and native plugins

---

## xaspx/hermes-control-interface

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
GitHub: https://github.com/xaspx/hermes-control-interface
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 713
A self-hosted web dashboard for the Hermes AI agent stack. Provides a browser-based terminal, file explorer, session overview, cron management, system metrics, and an agent status panel — all behind a

The Hermes Control Interface is a self-hosted web dashboard designed to manage the Hermes AI agent stack through a centralized graphical interface. It provides a multi-agent gateway to start, stop, and configure various agent profiles while monitoring real-time system metrics and token analytics. Users can interact with agents via a revamped chat interface featuring tool-call visualization, manage scheduled tasks through a cron manager, and edit files using a secure browser-based explorer. The platform includes robust security features like role-based access control, CSRF protection, and encrypted authentication.

Highlights:
- Multi-agent gateway for managing and configuring several Hermes profiles
- Real-time chat interface with tool-call cards and session resumption
- Comprehensive token analytics tracking costs, models, and platform usage

---

## stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
GitHub: https://github.com/stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 614
Lossless Context Management plugin for Hermes Agent — DAG-based context engine that never loses a message

Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard flat summarization with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that compacts older messages while preserving their underlying details in a SQLite store. This architecture allows the agent to use specialized tools like grep and expand to drill back into specific historical data without flooding the active context window. The system also includes safety features such as sensitive pattern redaction and externalization of large payloads to maintain performance.

Highlights:
- Hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware context compaction
- SQLite message store preserves raw history for precise retrieval
- Agent tools for searching and expanding compacted conversation nodes

---

## jazzyalex/agent-sessions

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/jazzyalex/agent-sessions
GitHub: https://github.com/jazzyalex/agent-sessions
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 588
Native macOS session browser, agent cockpit, analytics, and limits tracker for Hermes agents and other coding-agent CLIs

Agent Sessions is a local-first macOS application that provides a unified interface for managing and searching history from various AI coding agents. It works by indexing local session folders and databases to allow users to inspect transcripts and browse visual outputs without uploading data to the cloud. The tool supports a wide range of integrations including Hermes, Claude, Cursor, and Codex, and features an Agent Cockpit for monitoring active iTerm2 sessions. Users can recover saved sessions and launch resume commands directly into Terminal, iTerm2, or Warp.

Highlights:
- Unified search and browsing for multiple AI agent histories
- Local-only indexing with no telemetry or remote logging
- Agent Cockpit for live monitoring of active iTerm2 sessions

---

## Agent-3-7/minions

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Agent-3-7/minions
GitHub: https://github.com/Agent-3-7/minions
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 555
Mission Control for Hermes agents: create, supervise, and review autonomous Hermes Agent work from one screen

Minions is a centralized management interface for Hermes Agent that replaces fragmented terminal sessions with a unified dashboard. It utilizes a Kanban-style board to track persistent agent sessions, allowing users to monitor reasoning, tool calls, and task progress in real time. The system enforces a human-in-the-loop workflow where agents propose completions that require manual verification before being marked as done. Users can also manage scheduled recurring jobs and browse agent-created files through a local-first SQLite architecture.

Highlights:
- Kanban board for tracking autonomous task status and review queues
- Live streaming of agent reasoning and tool execution
- Human-in-the-loop verification for all agent-proposed task completions

---

## AaronWong1999/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/AaronWong1999/hermesclaw
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 555
Run Hermes Agent and OpenClaw on the same WeChat account

HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It acts as a central iLink poller and routes messages through local proxy servers to prevent the 403 errors and connection drops caused by multiple gateways competing for the same API token. This architecture enables users to switch between different AI brains or activate multiple agents at once using simple chat commands. It also introduces WeChat support for OpenCode, enabling voice-based coding tasks and access to free models like MiniMax M2.5.

Highlights:
- Runs three AI agents simultaneously on one WeChat account
- Prevents iLink token conflicts via local proxy routing
- Enables agent switching with /hermes, /openclaw, and /opencode commands

---

## swarmclawai/swarmclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/swarmclawai/swarmclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/swarmclawai/swarmclaw
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 532
Build autonomous AI agent swarms with orchestration, skills, and multiple model providers

SwarmClaw is a self-hosted AI agent runtime and multi-agent framework designed to orchestrate autonomous swarms. It operates by managing agent heartbeats, delegation, and durable memory across more than 23 LLM providers, including Hermes, Ollama, and Anthropic. Users can build complex workflows using a visual protocol builder and conversation-to-skill learning. The platform also integrates with external CLI tools like Claude Code and supports MCP tools for extended functionality.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates autonomous swarms with durable memory and delegation
- Supports 23+ LLM providers including Hermes and local Ollama
- Visual protocol builder for designing structured multi-agent workflows

---

## marshallrichards/ClawPhone

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/marshallrichards/ClawPhone
GitHub: https://github.com/marshallrichards/ClawPhone
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 529
Scripts and notes for running agent CLIs including Hermes Agent on Android smartphones through Termux

ClawPhone provides scripts and documentation for running AI agent command-line interfaces, such as Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, natively on Android devices via Termux. The project focuses on maintaining a native environment by patching binary assumptions and using glibc wrappers rather than relying on heavy virtualization layers. It includes an automated installer for Google's Antigravity CLI and detailed configurations for integrating phone hardware through Termux:API and Termux:GUI. This setup allows users to deploy isolated agent sandboxes on mobile hardware that can interact with system functions or external platforms like Discord.

Highlights:
- Automated installer for Antigravity CLI on ARM64 Android devices
- Native Termux deployment avoiding heavy proot or compatibility layers
- Hardware integration support via Termux:API and Termux:GUI extensions

---

## shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room
GitHub: https://github.com/shannhk/hermes-agent-control-room
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 489
Control Room-first template for scaling Hermes Agent from one VPS agent to specialist teams and automated workflows

Hermes Agent Control Room is a template-based framework designed to organize and govern Hermes agents across VPS environments. It functions as a sidecar control plane that provides a structured system map, registry, and runbook library to manage agent operations. The architecture supports scaling from a single agent to specialized teams using a task bus pattern for delegation and synthesis. Users can interact with agents directly or through an optional orchestrator that serves as a central front door for automated workflows.

Highlights:
- Level-based architecture for scaling from single agents to automated teams
- Task bus pattern for orchestrator-to-specialist delegation and handoffs
- Bundled skills for VPS provisioning, security auditing, and backup management

---

## nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1
GitHub: https://github.com/nickvasilescu/hermes-desktop-os1
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 449
Native macOS workspace for Hermes Agent running on Orgo cloud computers and SSH hosts

Hermes Desktop - OS1 Edition is a native macOS workspace designed to manage Hermes Agents running on Orgo cloud computers or SSH hosts. It provides a unified interface for VM provisioning, one-click agent installation, and real-time terminal access via websockets. The application includes built-in tools for session management, Kanban boards, and file editing with conflict checks. Additionally, it features a WebRTC-based voice mode that integrates OpenAI's Realtime API with Orgo MCP tools for voice-controlled agent interactions.

Highlights:
- Native macOS interface for Orgo cloud VMs and SSH hosts
- One-click automated Hermes Agent installation on fresh cloud computers
- Integrated WebRTC voice mode using OpenAI Realtime and Orgo MCP

## Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/Salomondiei08/oh-my-hermes
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 418
Opinionated workflow layer for building, shipping, and operating apps with Hermes Agent

Oh My Hermes is an opinionated workflow layer that transforms the Hermes Agent into an autonomous system for building and operating software projects. It provides a curated set of 23 skills and 6 specialized agents that allow Hermes to orchestrate the entire application lifecycle—from requirement clarification to deployment—directly from chat interfaces like Telegram or Slack. The system manages persistent memory and automated cron jobs to handle tasks such as issue triage, security scanning, and health monitoring. It integrates with external tools like Vercel, Supabase, and GitHub, while offering optional support for Claude Code and Codex for complex file editing.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates full application lifecycles from requirements to production deployment
- Includes 23 autonomous skills and 6 specialized agent roles
- Automates GitHub issue triage and security scanning via hourly loops

---

## joeynyc/hermes-skins

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/joeynyc/hermes-skins
GitHub: https://github.com/joeynyc/hermes-skins
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 404
Community CLI skins and themes for Hermes terminal UI

Hermes Skins is a collection of community-contributed visual themes for the Hermes CLI agent. The project uses YAML configuration files to customize terminal aesthetics including banner colors, ASCII art, spinner animations, and branding text without altering the agent's underlying logic. Users can apply themes for a session or permanently by placing files in a local directory, with missing values automatically inheriting from a default template. Available styles range from retro computing and cyberpunk aesthetics to minimalist monochrome and anime-inspired designs.

Highlights:
- Customizes CLI visuals including ASCII banners and spinner animations
- Uses YAML files for easy theme configuration and sharing
- Inherits from default skin for partial configuration support

---

## OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide
GitHub: https://github.com/OnlyTerp/hermes-optimization-guide
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 363
Performance optimization guide for Hermes deployments

The Hermes Optimization Guide is a comprehensive documentation and resource repository designed to help users deploy and scale Hermes Agent in production environments. It provides 24 instructional parts alongside runnable artifacts, including 13 installable skills, 5 pre-configured YAML templates, and a one-command VPS bootstrap script. The guide covers advanced implementations such as multi-platform orchestration across 22+ services, MCP server integration, and cost-optimized model routing using providers like Anthropic, OpenAI, and local deployments. It also includes reference architectures for various use cases ranging from homelabs to small agencies.

Highlights:
- One-command VPS bootstrap script for automated production-ready deployment
- 13 installable skills and 5 opinionated configuration templates
- Support for 22+ platforms including Telegram, Discord, and Slack

---

## Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
GitHub: https://github.com/Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 339
Meta-skill plugin that watches workflows and auto-generates reusable skills

Skill Factory is a meta-skill for Hermes Agent that automates the creation of reusable skills by monitoring user workflows. It functions by tracking session patterns in the background and proposing structured skill definitions when it detects repeatable sequences. Users can generate both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds directly from these proposals via slash commands or natural language. This tool streamlines the expansion of the Hermes ecosystem by turning manual environment setups and development tasks into permanent, executable assets.

Highlights:
- Automatically detects and proposes reusable skills from active workflows
- Generates both SKILL.md documentation and Python plugin scaffolds
- Provides slash commands to manage, list, and save detected patterns

---

## Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server
GitHub: https://github.com/Shelpuk-AI-Technology-Consulting/kindly-web-search-mcp-server
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 337
Web search and content retrieval MCP server supporting Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and other AI tools

Kindly Web Search is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI agents with deep content retrieval from the web, specifically optimized for coding tasks. It uses a headless browser and direct API integrations with platforms like GitHub, StackExchange, and arXiv to return full conversations and structured data rather than just snippets. By delivering complete context such as issue comments and upvoted answers in a single call, it eliminates the need for secondary scraping steps. The server integrates with tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and Hermes Agent to improve the quality of AI-generated code and documentation lookups.

Highlights:
- Retrieves full GitHub issues and StackOverflow threads in one call
- Uses headless browsing for real-time parsing of any webpage
- Supports Serper, Tavily, and self-hosted SearXNG as search providers

---

## DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
GitHub: https://github.com/DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 300
Type-safe agentskills.io support with progressive disclosure for Pydantic AI

pydantic-ai-skills is a standardized framework for managing modular agent capabilities within the Pydantic AI ecosystem. It implements the Agent Skills open specification by providing a tool-calling mechanism that allows agents to discover and execute specialized scripts or instructions. The library uses a progressive disclosure approach, loading skill details only when relevant to minimize token consumption. Developers can define these skills through local filesystem directories or programmatically using Python decorators and dataclasses.

Highlights:
- Implements the Agent Skills open specification for Pydantic AI
- Reduces token usage through progressive disclosure of skill metadata
- Supports both filesystem-based and programmatic Python skill definitions

---

## clawshell/clawshell

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawshell/clawshell
GitHub: https://github.com/clawshell/clawshell
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 288
The Runtime Security Layer for OpenClaw/Hermes-agent, the essential safety harness for PII & sensitive credentials protection.

ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provides secure email isolation through IMAP sender filtering and supports OAuth-based authentication for providers like OpenAI.

Highlights:
- Maps virtual keys to real credentials stored in protected Unix directories
- Scans and redacts PII in request and response bodies via regex
- Enforces sender-based email filtering for secure mailbox access

---

## stainlu/hermes-labyrinth

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/stainlu/hermes-labyrinth
GitHub: https://github.com/stainlu/hermes-labyrinth
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 277
Read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent with journeys, crossings, guideposts, and reports

Hermes Labyrinth is a read-only observability plugin for Hermes Agent that visualizes autonomous workflows as a structured map of events. It functions as a black-box recorder, reading local state to track prompts, tool calls, model transitions, and subagent activities without mutating session data. The tool provides deep diagnostic visibility through specialized views for skill overrides, scheduled cron tasks, and redacted journey reports. Users can export evidence in Markdown or JSON formats while relying on built-in redaction to protect sensitive credentials.

Highlights:
- Visualizes agent journeys through ordered crossings and event maps
- Provides diagnostic views for skill overrides and cron schedules
- Generates redacted Markdown and JSON reports for session evidence

---

## jwangkun/hermes-agent-guide

Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 277
A systematic Chinese-language guide to Hermes Agent — 16 books, 300K+ words, covering installation, advanced development, single- and multi-agent orchestration.

The Hermes Agent White Paper is a comprehensive Chinese-language technical guide designed to bridge the documentation gap for the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book curriculum covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory system, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details practical implementations including MCP protocol automation, multi-platform deployment on WeChat and Discord, and migration paths from OpenClaw. Users can follow specialized reading tracks for rapid deployment, deep technical development, or commercial application scenarios.

Highlights:
- 16-book systematic guide with over 300,000 words of technical content
- Deep analysis of five-layer architecture and three-tier memory systems
- Step-by-step integration for 15+ platforms and 6,000+ MCP services

## elkimek/honcho-self-hosted

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
GitHub: https://github.com/elkimek/honcho-self-hosted
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 269
Self-hosted Honcho memory backend for cross-session persistence

This project provides a configuration layer for self-hosting Plastic Labs' Honcho memory backend, enabling cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent without relying on third-party cloud storage. It deploys the full Honcho stack—including the API, PostgreSQL with pgvector, and Redis—on local hardware using Docker Compose. Users can route memory extraction and reasoning tasks through any OpenAI-compatible API or local inference servers like Ollama to maintain data sovereignty. This setup allows Hermes to build long-term user profiles and logical observations while keeping conversation data on the user's own infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Enables private cross-session memory for Hermes Agent via self-hosting
- Supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider or local inference servers
- Includes automated setup script for Docker, PostgreSQL, and Redis

---

## ucsandman/DashClaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ucsandman/DashClaw
GitHub: https://github.com/ucsandman/DashClaw
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 268
Decision infrastructure with guard policies and risk assessment for agents

DashClaw is a governance and decision infrastructure layer designed to secure AI agents interacting with real-world systems. It intercepts risky agent actions to evaluate them against declarative policies, routing high-risk tasks for human approval via a dashboard or mobile interface. The system records verifiable evidence for every decision and tracks terminal outcomes to prevent double-execution during agent retries. It integrates with various runtimes including Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and LangChain through MCP servers, SDKs, or lifecycle hooks.

Highlights:
- Evaluates risky agent actions against declarative guard policies
- Routes pending approvals to CLI, mobile, or messaging platforms
- Prevents double-execution by tracking durable terminal outcomes

---

Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 231
YouTube transcript, search, channel, and playlist skills for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf

YouTube Skills is a collection of tools that enables AI agents to interact with YouTube content without relying on browser automation or local binaries. It utilizes the TranscriptAPI backend to bypass cloud IP blocks, providing agents with direct access to video transcripts, search results, and channel metadata. The project integrates with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code using the Agent Skills format. Users can perform tasks like summarizing videos, extracting playlist contents, and searching for specific channel uploads through natural language prompts.

Highlights:
- Extracts transcripts, captions, and timestamps without using yt-dlp
- Supports video search, channel browsing, and playlist extraction
- Compatible with Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and Claude Code

## clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp
GitHub: https://github.com/clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 226
React SPA dashboard for Telegram Mini App v2.0 — 10-page mobile-first UI. Runs locally.

The Hermes Telegram Mini App is a React-based terminal-style interface that allows users to manage and interact with Hermes agents directly within Telegram. It functions as a mobile-first dashboard that connects to a local Hermes installation via a secure gateway, providing a suite of tools for agent control and system monitoring. The application features a streaming chat interface with vision and OCR support, a cron job manager for scheduled tasks, and a status tab for real-time hardware resource tracking. Security is handled through dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation to ensure only authorized Telegram users can access the agent.

Highlights:
- Terminal-style chat with streaming responses and slash command support
- Background agent spawning with live output monitoring and interactive modes
- Integrated management for creating, pausing, and triggering scheduled cron jobs

## raulvidis/hermes-android

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-android
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-android
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 223
Android device control — bridge app + Python toolset for mobile automation

Hermes-android is a remote device control bridge that enables AI agents to interact with Android hardware and software. It establishes a WebSocket connection between a Kotlin-based mobile app and a Python toolset on the server, allowing commands to bypass NAT and firewalls without port forwarding. The system provides 38 specialized tools for UI manipulation, media control, and system functions like SMS or GPS. It is also compatible with Android Automotive OS, extending agent capabilities to vehicle head units.

Highlights:
- Provides 38 tools for UI interaction, navigation, and system automation
- Uses AccessibilityService for screen reading and remote input execution
- Connects via WebSocket using a simple 6-character pairing code

---

## 335234131/agent-browser-mcp

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
GitHub: https://github.com/335234131/agent-browser-mcp
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 218
让 Agent 直接操作真实 Chrome 的 MCP 服务，支持页面扫描、CDP、截图与物理输入

Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, including real mouse movements, keyboard inputs, and desktop screenshots.

Highlights:
- Operates real Chrome sessions with preserved login states and cookies
- Executes native CDP commands for advanced DOM and browser control
- Supports physical mouse and keyboard inputs for human-like automation

---

## kaminocorp/hermes-alpha

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
GitHub: https://github.com/kaminocorp/hermes-alpha
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 210
Cloud-deployed Hermes with pre-configured templates and managed infrastructure

Hermes Alpha is an experimental framework designed to test if a stock AI agent can autonomously bootstrap and manage a bug bounty system from first principles. The architecture utilizes a two-agent loop where a persistent 'Overseer' agent builds, deploys, and refines an ephemeral 'Hunter' agent tasked with finding software vulnerabilities. By providing the agent with only a Linux terminal and an identity document rather than custom tools, the project evaluates the self-improvement capabilities of the Hermes model. The system integrates with Fly.io for managed infrastructure and uses Elephantasm for long-term memory streams.

Highlights:
- Autonomous two-agent loop for self-improving vulnerability research
- Web-based terminal interface with persistent WebSocket sessions
- Managed deployment of sub-agents via Fly.io infrastructure

---

HermesHub is a curated skills registry for Hermes Agent that provides a secure way to browse, install, and share AI agent capabilities. It utilizes the agentskills.io open standard and an automated security scanner that checks for threats like prompt injection and data exfiltration. The platform includes a creator marketplace supporting x402 and Micropayment protocols for premium skills, as well as an agent-to-agent feedback system for trust scoring.

Highlights:
- Automated security scanning with 65+ threat detection rules
- Creator marketplace with crypto and Stripe-based payment protocols
- Agent-to-agent feedback system for reliability and trust scores

Hermes Agent CN Desktop is a cross-platform client designed to simplify the deployment and management of Hermes Agent for Chinese-speaking users. Built with Tauri, Rust, and React, the application provides a native shell that manages local processes, file dialogs, and a secure REST/SSE proxy layer to bypass browser limitations. It features a managed runtime environment that handles the installation, health monitoring, and updating of the underlying Hermes Agent core. Users can access a comprehensive interface for managing skills, memory, MCP tools, and model provider configurations through a unified desktop workbench.

## robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
GitHub: https://github.com/robbyczgw-cla/hermes-web-search-plus
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 200
Multi-provider web search (Serper, Tavily, Exa, Querit, Perplexity) with auto-routing

Web-search-plus is a multi-provider search and URL extraction plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It uses a capability-based routing engine called Routing v2 to automatically select the best available search provider based on the user's configured API keys. The tool supports over a dozen backends including Serper, Tavily, and Exa, while offering clean content extraction through dedicated providers like Firecrawl and Linkup. It features a standalone setup wizard with presets to help users manage multiple API keys and configure provider priorities without manual environment file editing.

Highlights:
- Adds web_search_plus and web_extract_plus tools to Hermes Agent
- Auto-routes queries across Serper, Tavily, Exa, and You.com
- Supports clean URL extraction via Linkup, Firecrawl, and Parallel

## Cranot/super-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Cranot/super-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/Cranot/super-hermes
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 200
Skills that teach Hermes Agent to write its own analytical prompts

Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis missed to ensure transparency in the agent's cognitive limits.

Highlights:
- Teaches agents to write custom thinking instructions for specific problems
- Identifies structural conservation laws and trade-offs in complex codebases
- Generates constraint reports detailing analysis blind spots and limitations

---

## nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel

GitHub: https://github.com/nativ3ai/hermes-agent-camel
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 185
Hermes with integrated CaMeL trust boundaries for safer autonomous execution

This project is a version of the Hermes Agent that integrates CaMeL-style trust boundaries to protect against indirect prompt injection. It implements an opt-in runtime guard that monitors or enforces security policies by gating sensitive tool side effects against verified user intent. The system maintains the core Hermes features, including a self-improving learning loop, autonomous skill creation, and cross-platform accessibility via Telegram, Discord, and Slack. Users can toggle between monitoring modes to record policy decisions or enforcement modes to block unauthorized actions during autonomous execution.

Highlights:
- Opt-in CaMeL runtime guard for indirect prompt-injection protection
- Self-improving learning loop with autonomous skill creation and memory
- Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and Signal

## ReinaMacCredy/maestro

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
GitHub: https://github.com/ReinaMacCredy/maestro
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 177
Harness for long-running AI agents — structured memory, cross-feature learning, plan-approve-execute

Maestro is a local-first agent harness that manages the spec-to-ship lifecycle through a structured on-disk state model. It uses a CLI to maintain shared state in a local directory, allowing separate agent sessions to collaborate without a central database or server. By persisting specs, tasks, and handoff envelopes as files, it ensures agents can resume work with durable continuation records and auditable evidence. The system enforces discipline through machine-checked contracts and a deterministic verification process that gates task completion.

Highlights:
- Maintains shared agent state locally in .maestro/ without a database
- Decomposes complex specs into multi-task execution plans and PRs
- Emits passive JSON handoff envelopes for seamless cross-session agent transitions

---

## yoloshii/ClawMem

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yoloshii/ClawMem
GitHub: https://github.com/yoloshii/ClawMem
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 177
On-device memory and context engine for agents — local-first, no cloud dependencies

ClawMem is a local-first memory and context engine that provides AI agents with persistent, on-device knowledge. It utilizes a hybrid architecture combining multi-signal retrieval, intent classification, and multi-graph traversal to surface relevant context from markdown notes and session transcripts. The system integrates with Claude Code, OpenClaw, and Hermes agents via a shared SQLite vault. It enables agents to capture decisions, generate session handoffs, and maintain a self-evolving memory layer without cloud dependencies.

Highlights:
- Local-first architecture with no API keys or cloud dependencies
- Hybrid retrieval using BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs
- Integrates via MCP server, Claude Code hooks, and agent plugins

---

## Signet-AI/signetai

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Signet-AI/signetai
GitHub: https://github.com/Signet-AI/signetai
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 176
Local-first identity, memory, and secrets layer for AI agents — portable state across Hermes Agent, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, and MCP clients

Signet-AI is a local-first context and memory layer designed to provide persistent identity and knowledge across different AI agent harnesses. It captures session history and workspace data in a local SQLite and markdown substrate, indexing it for retrieval through hybrid search and graph traversal. This architecture allows users to maintain continuity and secrets when switching between tools like Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw. By decoupling memory from specific model providers, it ensures that agent context remains inspectable, portable, and under the user's direct control.

Highlights:
- Local-first storage using SQLite and markdown for data ownership
- Cross-harness continuity for Hermes Agent, Claude Code, and OpenClaw
- Hybrid retrieval combining vector search, FTS5, and graph traversal

## 42-evey/hermes-plugins

GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/hermes-plugins
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 159
Goal management, inter-agent bridge, model selection, and cost control plugins

Evey's Hermes Plugins is a collection of 23 custom extensions designed to enhance the autonomy, observability, and cost-efficiency of the Hermes Agent framework. The suite implements specialized logic for multi-model decision debates, smart task routing with fallback chains, and structured telemetry logging. These tools enable agents to manage their own goals, perform self-correction loops, and consolidate long-term memory into vector storage. Additionally, the project includes a bidirectional bridge for Claude Code and budget enforcement via Langfuse integration.

Highlights:
- Autonomous planning, reflection, and multi-model consensus debate tools
- Smart model routing with sensitivity filters and four-model fallback chains
- Automated memory consolidation into Qdrant vectors and identity evolution

## tlehman/litprog-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/tlehman/litprog-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/tlehman/litprog-skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 158
Literate programming skill — weave code and documentation across agents

The litprog-skill is a Claude Code extension that implements Donald Knuth's literate programming paradigm by transforming codebases into human-readable narratives. It works by generating a .lit.md file that weaves together prose, Mermaid diagrams, and LaTeX math with the original source code. The tool includes a tangler to extract runnable files and a reverse-sync engine to ensure the documentation stays updated when source files are edited. This approach helps developers document architectural decisions and facilitates easier onboarding through structured, essay-like code explanations.

Highlights:
- Generates .lit.md files combining prose, diagrams, and source code
- Extracts runnable source files from documentation using a tangler
- Automatically syncs source file edits back to the literate document

---

Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 145
Glassmorphic web interface for Hermes Agent — your self-hosted AI assistant

Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web interface that serves as a centralized command center for managing self-hosted Hermes Agents. It operates as a single-file React application connected to a lightweight Python proxy server to facilitate real-time communication and system monitoring. Users can interact through a multi-panel dashboard featuring chat, live log streaming, file browsing, and memory inspection. The interface also supports specialized workflows including cron job scheduling, MCP tool management, and workspace-based video uploads.

Highlights:
- Single-file React 18 application with a lightweight Python proxy server
- Integrated file browser, terminal, and real-time log streaming interface
- Management of MCP tools, skill browsing, and scheduled cron jobs
