## plur-ai/plur

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/plur-ai/plur
GitHub: https://github.com/plur-ai/plur
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 144
Shared memory layer with open engram YAML format for multi-agent systems

PLUR is a persistent, local-first memory layer designed to synchronize agent corrections, preferences, and conventions across different tools and sessions. It stores knowledge as plain YAML "engrams" on disk and uses a hybrid BM25 and embedding-based retrieval system to inject relevant context into agent prompts. The system employs an ACT-R activation model where facts strengthen with use and naturally decay over time to prevent context clutter. It integrates directly with MCP-compatible tools, Claude Code, and Hermes Agent to automate the capture and recall of project-specific knowledge.

Highlights:
- Stores memory locally as open-format YAML engrams with zero API costs
- Uses ACT-R activation to strengthen relevant facts and decay stale ones
- Provides MCP tools for automated learning and hybrid context injection

---

It operates through a Tkinter-based GUI or CLI, utilizing an embedded Python environment to connect with local models via LM Studio or cloud providers like OpenRouter. The agent features a library of 100 tools for tasks including music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation. Users can also build custom automation pipelines through a built-in workflow engine and a dynamic tool maker for wrapping REST APIs.

- Dynamic tool maker and workflow engine for custom automation pipelines

It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine for multi-step automation. Users can also extend the agent's capabilities at runtime using a built-in dynamic tool maker for API wrapping and Python scripting.

Category: Guides & Docs

A 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building an autonomous AI agent from zero in Python — agent loop, tool system, memory, skills, MCP, multi-platform gateway, and self-evolution.

Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementations for each stage to help users understand the design backbone of autonomous systems.

Highlights:
- Step-by-step implementation of agent loops and tool dispatch systems
- Multi-platform gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WeChat
- Advanced memory management with context compression and session persistence

## esaradev/icarus-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/esaradev/icarus-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/esaradev/icarus-plugin
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 122
Self-memory and replacement models — remember your work, train your replacement

Icarus is a Hermes plugin designed to create self-improving agents through shared memory and automated model fine-tuning. It captures agent decisions, session outcomes, and structured notes into a markdown-based "fabric" directory that can be optionally managed via Obsidian. This data is then used to extract high-quality training pairs for fine-tuning cheaper replacement models via Together AI. The system includes a full pipeline for evaluating candidate models against base versions and switching to them once performance thresholds are met.

Highlights:
- Cross-instance shared memory via markdown-based fabric directories
- Automated training data extraction with decision-quality tagging
- End-to-end pipeline for fine-tuning and evaluating replacement models

---

## AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow

GitHub: https://github.com/AkoliteZA/hermes-agent-idea-workflow
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 121
Pre-build idea-to-spec workflow skills for Hermes Agent that turn rough ideas into design docs and implementation handoffs

The Hermes Agent Idea Workflow is a suite of skills designed to transform vague product concepts into structured, build-ready specifications. It operates through a staged pipeline that separates product intent from technical implementation, utilizing guided interviews and a reusable question bank to generate markdown artifacts. The system supports both a Lite mode for quick capture and a Full mode that produces comprehensive design docs, UI briefs, and implementation specs. These outputs are specifically formatted to serve as direct handoff material for downstream coding workflows like Superpowers for GPT.

Highlights:
- Converts rough ideas into structured design and implementation documents
- Features Lite and Full modes for varying project complexity
- Includes a spec review gate with PASS/FAIL evaluation models

## 1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent

Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 119
17 specialized agents for research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure

Hermes is a multi-agent orchestration system built for OpenCode AI to manage complex software development lifecycles. It utilizes a master orchestrator to route tasks through specialized pipelines involving 17 distinct agents focused on research, planning, implementation, quality, and infrastructure. The system enforces mandatory quality gates, such as automated reviews and testing, while managing context flow between agents to ensure consistency. It integrates with MCP servers for external documentation retrieval and supports specific workflows for bug fixing, refactoring, and security-sensitive features.

Highlights:
- Coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains
- Enforces mandatory quality and security gates for code changes
- Automates task routing through predefined research and implementation pipelines

## linke-ai/hermes-agent-team

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/linke-ai/hermes-agent-team
GitHub: https://github.com/linke-ai/hermes-agent-team
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 119
Local multi-agent team collaboration web system built on Hermes Agent profiles, MCP, ACP, and Hermes Kanban

Hermes Agents Team is a local web-based system designed to orchestrate multi-agent collaboration using Hermes Agent profiles. It utilizes a Leader/Specialist architecture to automate task decomposition, assignment, and review through a centralized Web UI and Hermes Kanban. The system integrates MCP and ACP protocols to manage agent communications, skills, and tool calls entirely within a local environment. Users can monitor real-time dialogue, terminal outputs, and task progression while maintaining full control over model configurations and data privacy.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates Leader and Specialist agents for automated task lifecycle management
- Integrates MCP and ACP protocols for local tool and communication handling
- Real-time Web UI for monitoring agent dialogues and Kanban task flows

---

## EfficientContext/ContextPilot

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
GitHub: https://github.com/EfficientContext/ContextPilot
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 112
Accelerating Long Context LLM Inference with Accuracy-Preserving Context Optimization in SGLang, vLLM, llama.cpp, OpenClaw, RAG, and Agentic AI.

ContextPilot is a context optimization engine designed to accelerate long-context LLM inference by maximizing KV cache reuse. It functions by maintaining a context index that reorders and deduplicates overlapping context blocks into shared prefixes before they reach the inference server. This approach reduces redundant token computation and prefill latency without compromising model reasoning quality. The project provides native integrations for Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, vLLM, and SGLang, supporting both local deployments and cloud APIs.

Highlights:
- Increases cache hits by 4–12× through prefix reordering and deduplication
- Reduces prefill latency by 1.5–3× across RAG and agentic workloads
- Native plugin support for Hermes Agent and OpenClaw ecosystems

---

## AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC
GitHub: https://github.com/AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 109
Hermes Agent ported to Claude Code Channel — 46 native skills, no OAuth, no external process

hermes-CCC is a port of the Hermes Agent framework specifically designed for the Claude Code native skill system. It integrates the Hermes brain architecture directly into the Claude Code execution context, eliminating the need for external processes or OAuth authentication. The project implements 46 core skills across categories such as MLOps, software development, and research, including specialized tools for RL training and vector database management. This allows developers to utilize Hermes-style memory, personas, and task routing while remaining within a unified terminal environment.

Highlights:
- Port of Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills to CCC
- Runs natively in Claude Code without separate processes or OAuth
- Includes MLOps tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and RL training

---

Stars: 104
Oracle network and smart contract interaction skills (agentskills.io spec)

This repository provides a collection of standardized coding skills for AI agents to interact with the Chainlink oracle network and smart contract ecosystem. Built on the agentskills.io specification, these tools allow agents to perform tasks such as managing cross-chain transfers, integrating data feeds, and implementing verifiable randomness. Developers can install specific skills via the Vercel open skills CLI to enable agents to generate workflows, handle on-chain verification, and manage runtime operations across multiple blockchain networks.

Highlights:
- Standardized skills for CCIP, Data Feeds, VRF, and Data Streams
- Supports CRE onboarding, workflow generation, and local simulations
- Compatible with Vercel's open skills ecosystem CLI for easy installation

## sanchomuzax/hermes-webui

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/sanchomuzax/hermes-webui
GitHub: https://github.com/sanchomuzax/hermes-webui
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 100
Process monitoring and configuration dashboard for Hermes

Hermes WebUI is a process monitoring and configuration dashboard designed to provide a visual interface for the Hermes Agent. It functions as a non-intrusive polling bridge that reads from the agent's SQLite database and YAML files without requiring modifications to the core agent code. Users can monitor real-time activity feeds, track costs, and inspect full conversation histories through a responsive web interface. The tool also provides read-only access to environment configurations and allows for the management of scheduled cron jobs and agent skills.

Highlights:
- Real-time dashboard for session activity, gateway status, and cost tracking
- Full conversation history inspection with FTS5 search and message-level detail
- Read-only configuration viewer and management interface for scheduled cron jobs

## howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
GitHub: https://github.com/howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 86
Meta-harness for Hermes Agent — meta-optimization with arxiv paper reference

Hermes Agent Meta-Harness is a standalone outer-loop optimization framework designed to improve the performance of Hermes agents on verifiable coding benchmarks. It treats the agent as an execution backend and searches for optimal harness configurations—the code governing context collection and retrieval—rather than modifying model weights. The system orchestrates benchmark evaluations, tracks performance frontiers, and performs structured candidate mutations to find superior harness variants. It currently supports TBLite and TB2 benchmarks through a research-safe workflow that emphasizes archive analysis and deterministic search.

Highlights:
- Optimizes agent harness code through automated outer-loop search
- Orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations via Hermes
- Tracks performance frontiers with structured candidate mutation and comparison

---

## unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
GitHub: https://github.com/unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 84
AI-native browser built for autonomous agent control via MCP

Vessel is an open-source, Chromium-based browser designed specifically for autonomous agent control rather than traditional human-first navigation. It operates as a persistent runtime where agents drive workflows via the Model Context Protocol (MCP) while humans monitor and intervene through a visible supervisory interface. The browser maintains durable state through named sessions, annotated checkpoints, and an action undo feature that rolls back the session to a state prior to the last agent action. It integrates with various LLM providers and agent harnesses like Hermes Agent to support long-running, auditable web tasks.

Highlights:
- Provides a visible UI for human supervision of autonomous agents
- Exposes browser control and page visibility via MCP endpoints
- Maintains durable state with session checkpoints and action rollback

---

## armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
GitHub: https://github.com/armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 80
Converts repos, docs, and developer discourse into agentskills.io-compliant skills

Skill Forge is a development tool that converts source code, documentation, and developer discussions into verified instruction files for AI agents. It uses Abstract Syntax Tree (AST) analysis and documentation scraping to extract precise function signatures, parameter types, and usage patterns. Every generated instruction includes a provenance map linking back to specific file line numbers at pinned commits or documentation URLs. This approach prevents AI hallucinations by providing agents with version-pinned, immutable skills that comply with the agentskills.io specification.

Highlights:
- Generates verified AI instructions from code repositories and documentation
- Provides traceability with direct links to source file line numbers
- Supports version-pinned skill management to prevent instruction rot

---

## ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart
GitHub: https://github.com/ultraworkers/hermes-agent-helm-chart
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 78
Unofficial community Helm chart for deploying Hermes Agent on Kubernetes with cloud-native state-safety guardrails

This unofficial community Helm chart facilitates the deployment of Nous Research's Hermes Agent on Kubernetes clusters. It provides a structured configuration model that enforces state-safety guardrails, such as single-replica constraints and specific update strategies, to prevent data corruption in persistent environments. The chart supports both direct workload management and an operator-ready mode for platform teams using custom resource definitions. It is optimized for multi-tenant isolation, offering granular control over networking, storage, and secret management for individual agent instances.

Highlights:
- Enforces single-writer state safety for persistent HERMES_HOME storage
- Supports direct deployment or operator-ready CRD-based workflows
- Configurable gateway mode with optional API and webhook listeners

---

The installer enables mobile access to over 70 built-in tools and supports integration with both cloud-based APIs and local models via Ollama.

- One-line automated installation script for Termux environments
- Supports local model execution using Ollama on Android
- Enables persistent memory and cross-platform tool execution

Pan is a self-hosted web dashboard and workspace designed specifically for the Hermes Agent runtime. It provides a centralized interface to manage agent interactions through a chat-first environment that supports SSE streaming and tool timelines. Users can discover and install skills from skills.sh, manage MCP integrations, and organize work into isolated profiles with unique memory and sessions. The system includes a setup wizard that automatically detects Hermes configurations and can run as a background daemon or system service.

Stars: 69
Registry of reusable AI skills based on AgentSkills specification

SkillDock is a registry and SDK for managing reusable AI skills based on the AgentSkills specification. It provides a Python client and CLI that interact with an OpenAPI-driven backend to discover, install, and version AI capabilities. The platform supports a commerce layer for buying and selling skills using Stripe or TON blockchain payments. Developers can package local skill folders, manage dependencies, and publish immutable releases with metadata defined in Markdown frontmatter.

Highlights:

- Supports skill monetization via Stripe and TON blockchain payment rails
- Automated skill installation with recursive dependency resolution and versioning

## Abruptive/Ankh.md

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Abruptive/Ankh.md
GitHub: https://github.com/Abruptive/Ankh.md
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 58
TAW Agent framework for creating scoped Hermes Agents

Ankh.md is an open-source framework designed to transform a single Hermes Agent into multiple specialized, project-specific instances. It works by detecting a local `.agent/` directory and dynamically overriding the default `hermes` command with a scoped agent configuration. This allows developers to maintain unique instructions, skills, and memories for different codebases or workflows without cross-project interference.

Highlights:
- Per-folder scoping with local configuration and skills
- Seamlessly switches between default and scoped Hermes agents
- Supports project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions

## adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman
GitHub: https://github.com/adityahimaone/hermes-agent-rtk-caveman
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 53
Hermes Agent RTK and Caveman setup with preconfigured skills and token-saving CLI workflows

This project provides a preconfigured setup for Hermes Agent that integrates Rust Token Killer (RTK) and Caveman templating to reduce token consumption during CLI operations. It works by wrapping standard terminal commands and outputting compact data structures, achieving a reported 90-99% reduction in token usage. The repository includes over 40 pre-configured skills covering finance management, GitHub workflows, research tools, and DevOps automation. Users can deploy the environment via an automated installer to gain immediate access to token-optimized aliases for git, linting, and testing tasks.

Highlights:
- Achieves 90-99% token savings using RTK and Caveman templating
- Includes 40+ pre-configured skills for finance, devops, and research
- Automated cross-platform installation for macOS and Linux systems

Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 51
Autonomous AI companies competing for GitHub stars — agent-vs-agent arena

Gladiator is an autonomous agent arena that pits two AI companies against each other to compete for GitHub stars on identical repositories. The system uses Paperclip for orchestration and Hermes Agent workers to execute tasks according to distinct corporate strategies: growth-focused Blitz versus quality-focused Craft. A real-time dashboard visualizes the competition through scoreboards, task boards, and audit trails. Upon completion, the project demonstrates cross-company skill transfer by merging the rival entities and their learned memories.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates 9 autonomous agents across two competing AI companies
- Uses Paperclip and Hermes Agent for task execution and learning
- Real-time dashboard visualizes agent tasks, code, and scoreboard metrics

## anneheartrecord/hermes-agent-anatomy

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/anneheartrecord/hermes-agent-anatomy
GitHub: https://github.com/anneheartrecord/hermes-agent-anatomy
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 49
Chinese Hermes Agent source-code anatomy and architecture analysis

This project provides a systematic technical decomposition and architectural analysis of the Hermes Agent source code. It breaks down the framework's 334,000 lines of Python code into modular guides covering the core agent loop, tool registration, and multi-provider adapters. The documentation includes visual technical illustrations and a 13-dimension comparative study against other frameworks like Claude Code and OpenClaw. Users can leverage these docs to understand complex features such as context compression, RL training data collection, and 11-platform message gateway integration.

Highlights:
- Detailed analysis of core agent cycles and iteration budget management
- Deep dives into context compression and structured LLM summarization techniques
- Technical breakdown of 8 memory backends and 11 message platform adapters

---

## TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
GitHub: https://github.com/TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 49
Hermes Agent sandboxed with hardware-level enforcement

HermesClaw is a deployment framework that runs the Hermes Agent within NVIDIA’s OpenShell sandbox to provide hardware-level security enforcement. It utilizes kernel-level mechanisms like Landlock LSM and Seccomp BPF to restrict the agent's filesystem access, network egress, and system calls. Users can toggle between strict and permissive policy presets to enable features like web searching or messaging integrations while maintaining an out-of-process security boundary. The system supports persistent memory, self-improving skills, and multi-platform communication via Telegram and Discord.

Highlights:
- Hardware-enforced sandboxing via NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking
- Three security policy presets ranging from strict inference to permissive web-access
- Pre-built skills library for research digests, code reviews, and market alerts

---

## mudrii/hermes-agent-docs

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/mudrii/hermes-agent-docs
GitHub: https://github.com/mudrii/hermes-agent-docs
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 49
Community documentation covering deployment patterns and v0.2.0+ workflows

Hermes Agent is an autonomous AI system designed for persistent learning and multi-platform task execution. It utilizes a closed-loop architecture that creates and refines skills from experience while maintaining long-term memory via SQLite and FTS5 session search. The agent operates across diverse environments including a messaging gateway for over 20 platforms, an Ink-based TUI, and an OpenAI-compatible API server. Its plugin architecture allows for modular extension of tools, providers, and transport layers without modifying the core codebase.

Highlights:
- Autonomous skill creation and self-improvement based on task experience
- Unified messaging gateway supporting Telegram, Discord, Slack, and WhatsApp
- Pluggable transport layer for Anthropic, Bedrock, OpenAI, and local providers

---

Stars: 40
Windows-native Hermes Agent adaptation with one-line PowerShell installer and no WSL requirement

This project is a Windows-native adaptation of the Hermes Agent that eliminates the need for WSL2 or Linux environments. It utilizes a PowerShell-based installer to deploy the agent directly into the Windows ecosystem while resolving platform-specific issues like process signal handling. The fork introduces a dedicated Web UI management panel for real-time chat, session history, and visual configuration of skills and API keys.

Highlights:
- Native Windows installation via a one-line PowerShell script
- Eliminates WSL2 requirement for running Hermes Agent
- Integrated Web UI for chat, logs, and token usage analytics

GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/evey-setup
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 39
Get a hermes-agent stack running in minutes — free models, 29 plugins, zero cost

Evey Setup is a deployment toolkit designed to bootstrap a complete autonomous AI agent environment based on the Hermes Agent framework. It uses a multi-phase shell script to orchestrate a containerized stack including model routing via LiteLLM, local GPU inference through Ollama, and vector memory with Qdrant. The installer provides three service tiers—Base, Services, and Full—allowing users to scale from a minimal agent to a production-ready system with monitoring and workflow automation. It integrates 29 community plugins and pre-configures free model routing to enable zero-cost operation.

Highlights:
- Deploys Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant via Docker
- Includes 29 community plugins for memory, social, and quality validation
- Automates configuration for free model routing and local GPU inference

GitHub: https://github.com/xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 38
Simple Docker sandbox image for Hermes Agent

This project provides a minimal Docker sandbox image designed to package and deploy Hermes Agent. It utilizes the official upstream installation script to set up the agent and includes mini-swe-agent within the container. The image supports multi-arch publishing and allows for persistent state management by mounting the agent's home directory. Users can easily build specific versions using build arguments to target different Hermes branches or tags.

Highlights:
- Includes mini-swe-agent and official Hermes installation
- Supports persistent state via mounted home directories

## teknium1/hermes-miniverse

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
GitHub: https://github.com/teknium1/hermes-miniverse
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 38
Bridge Hermes to Miniverse pixel worlds — agent embodiment in virtual environments

Hermes-miniverse is a bridge that enables Hermes Agents to inhabit and interact within Miniverse pixel worlds. It functions through a three-part architecture consisting of a standalone bridge daemon, a gateway hook for state broadcasting, and a specific skill that allows the agent to consciously navigate the environment. This integration allows agents to maintain a visual presence, update their status in real-time, and engage in bidirectional communication with other AI entities. Users can deploy multiple bridges simultaneously to populate a virtual world with specialized Hermes instances.

Highlights:
- Connects Hermes Agents to Miniverse pixel-based virtual environments
- Synchronizes live agent states including working, thinking, and idle
- Enables bidirectional messaging between Hermes and other world agents

## roli-lpci/lintlang

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/roli-lpci/lintlang
GitHub: https://github.com/roli-lpci/lintlang
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 38
Static linter for AI agent configs, tool descriptions, and system prompts. HERM v1.1 scoring.

lintlang is a static analysis tool designed to identify structural and linguistic failures in AI agent configurations, system prompts, and tool definitions. It utilizes regex and AST-based heuristics rather than LLM calls to detect issues like vague tool descriptions and missing termination conditions. This approach ensures deterministic, zero-cost scans that run in approximately 2ms per file. The tool is intended for use in CI/CD pipelines to prevent prompt drift and logic errors before runtime.

Highlights:
- Deterministic structural analysis using 7 specific detector patterns (H1–H7)
- Zero LLM calls for cost-effective and offline CI integration
- Supports YAML, JSON, plain text, and .prompt file formats

---

## Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander
GitHub: https://github.com/Lethe044/hermes-incident-commander
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 37
Autonomous SRE agent — detects, heals, and learns from production incidents

Hermes Incident Commander is an autonomous SRE agent designed to automate the detection, diagnosis, and remediation of production infrastructure failures. It utilizes the Hermes Agent framework to monitor system vitals, classify incident severity, and apply fixes across CPU, memory, disk, and service layers. Following resolution, the agent generates structured post-mortem reports and automatically creates new prevention skills to handle similar future incidents more efficiently. The system integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time alerting and uses cron scheduling for continuous health audits.

Highlights:
- Automates incident triage, root cause analysis, and remediation actions.
- Generates persistent prevention skills and post-incident reports automatically.
- Integrates with Telegram and Discord for real-time P0 alerts.

## metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu
GitHub: https://github.com/metantonio/hermes-wsl-ubuntu
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 37
Step-by-step WSL2 Ubuntu setup guide for Windows users

This project provides a comprehensive setup guide and automation scripts for running Hermes Agent locally on Windows via WSL2 or natively on macOS. It integrates llama.cpp for model inference with the Qwen3.5 model family, utilizing GPU acceleration through CUDA or Metal. The guide covers the entire stack including the Camofox browser server for web-browsing tasks and the Hermes HUDUI for a graphical user interface. By following this documentation, users can deploy a private, local agentic environment capable of browser automation and LLM interaction.

Highlights:
- Automated installation scripts for Ubuntu (WSL2) and macOS platforms
- Configures llama.cpp with CUDA or Metal for GPU-accelerated inference
- Integrates Camofox browser server for autonomous web-browsing capabilities

## yepyhun/Brainstack

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yepyhun/Brainstack
GitHub: https://github.com/yepyhun/Brainstack
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 37
Memory kernel stack for Hermes agent

Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to replace flat memory blobs in Hermes agents with a structured, multi-layered memory model. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to handle session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. By categorizing information into specific "shelves" like profile, graph, and corpus, it prevents prompt stuffing and ensures Hermes receives only the most relevant, bounded evidence for each query. The system includes a dedicated installer and diagnostic tools to maintain stateful conversation continuity within the Hermes-Agent runtime.

Highlights:
- Consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval
- Uses SQLite, Kuzu, and Chroma for multi-layered data storage
- Provides bounded evidence packing to reduce prompt noise

---

## sheawinkler/hermes-agent-ultra

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/sheawinkler/hermes-agent-ultra
GitHub: https://github.com/sheawinkler/hermes-agent-ultra
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 36
Rust-based Hermes Agent Ultra derivative focused on performance and Hermes Agent feature parity

Hermes Agent Ultra is a Rust-native autonomous agent runtime designed to provide functional parity with the original Hermes Agent while adding advanced reliability and operator controls. It replaces Python-based execution paths with deterministic Rust implementations to improve performance and security across local and gateway sessions. The system features a comprehensive tool runtime with policy enforcement, session time-travel for debugging, and a multi-provider router supporting backends like Ollama and vLLM. Users can manage complex agent workflows through a TUI or CLI with built-in support for memory fusion and automated diagnostics.

Highlights:
- Rust-native core for deterministic execution and high-performance agent loops
- Advanced session branching and time-travel for checkpointing and replaying traces
- Multi-backend support including Ollama, llama.cpp, vLLM, and Apple ANE

---

## keepnotes-ai/keep

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/keepnotes-ai/keep
GitHub: https://github.com/keepnotes-ai/keep
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 35
Reflective memory for AI agents

Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actions and current intentions.

Highlights:
- Semantic search with BM25 full-text and ranked graph traversal
- Automated indexing for local directories, URLs, and multimedia files
- Edge tags create navigable relationships with automatic inverse links

---

## Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill
GitHub: https://github.com/Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 34
ACP-style multi-agent delegation — routes tasks to Hermes, Codex, or Claude Code

The hermes-acp-orchestrator is a skill designed to standardize multi-agent delegation within engineering workflows. It functions by routing specific goals to specialized agents including Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while maintaining strict context isolation. The system ensures operational stability through configurable safety controls such as execution timeouts and output character limits. This tool is primarily intended for complex tasks requiring distinct phases like implementation, risk analysis, and final synthesis.

Highlights:
- Routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code.
- Prevents parent context pollution through isolated delegation workflows.
- Enforces safety via configurable timeouts and output character limits.

---

## Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop
GitHub: https://github.com/Felix-Forever/hermes-agent-desktop
Category: Workspaces & GUIs
Stars: 34
Multi-agent Hermes Agent desktop client with specialists, visual skill store, PM orchestrator, and streaming chat

Hermes Agent Desktop is a native GUI client that transforms the Hermes Agent CLI into a multi-agent collaborative workspace. It utilizes a Project Manager orchestrator to decompose complex user requirements into sub-tasks, which are then delegated to a team of 20 specialized AI agents including architects, engineers, and designers. The application features a visual Skill Store for one-click installation of over 50 tools and provides a streaming chat interface with rich markdown support. It is built using pywebview and supports any OpenAI-compatible LLM provider for flexible model switching.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates 20 specialized AI agents through a Project Manager system
- Integrated visual Skill Store with 50+ one-click installable tools
- Native desktop interface with workspace management and model switching

---

## cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
GitHub: https://github.com/cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 33
Progressive MCP client with three-layer lazy loading — validates agentskills.io pattern

Agentic-MCP-Skill is an experimental MCP client designed to optimize token consumption through a three-layer lazy loading architecture. It implements a progressive disclosure pattern that fetches server metadata, tool lists, and specific schemas only when required by the agent. By utilizing a socket-based daemon to manage persistent connections, it avoids the overhead of loading entire toolsets into the AI context at once. This approach is particularly effective for complex MCP servers like Playwright where only a subset of tools is needed for a given task.

Highlights:
- Three-layer progressive disclosure reduces token usage by up to 86%
- Socket-based daemon architecture maintains persistent MCP server connections
- Supports hot-reloading configurations without restarting the background process

---

## Christabel337/job-scout-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Christabel337/job-scout-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/Christabel337/job-scout-agent
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 33
Autonomous job hunting — scans listings, writes cover letters, tracks applications

Job Scout Agent is an automated job search tool designed to streamline the discovery and tracking of employment opportunities. The agent utilizes Hermes Agent capabilities to fetch live listings from job board APIs, score them against user-defined profiles, and perform company research. It organizes findings into structured local directories and maintains a CSV tracker for easy integration with Google Sheets. This system eliminates manual searching by providing a daily summary of relevant roles with direct application links.

Highlights:
- Fetches live job listings using browser-based API integration
- Scores roles against skills, salary, and location preferences
- Maintains an automated CSV tracker for application management

---

## bigph00t/hermescraft

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/bigph00t/hermescraft
GitHub: https://github.com/bigph00t/hermescraft
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 33
Embodied AI companion for Minecraft — persistent memory, vision, learning, multi-agent

HermesCraft is an embodied AI framework that integrates Hermes agents into Minecraft as persistent, interactive players. It connects the Hermes agent stack to a Mineflayer bot body through a custom Minecraft CLI and HTTP API, allowing agents to perceive and interact with the game world. Agents can function as individual companions or as part of multi-agent civilizations with private messaging and social memory. The system emphasizes fair-play perception by restricting agent knowledge to line-of-sight visuals and directional audio rather than using privileged game data.

Highlights:
- Embodied agents with persistent memory, identity, and tool-use capabilities
- Fair-play perception system using line-of-sight filtering and spatial mapping
- Multi-agent social features including public chat and private messaging

---

## 0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes
Category: Developer Tools
Stars: 32
Migration tool from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent

openclaw-to-hermes is a migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. It automates the transfer of agent personas, workspace files, model configurations, and credentials between the two ecosystems. The tool addresses specific limitations found in earlier migration methods, such as incorrect authentication formatting and incomplete workspace detection. It provides a safe migration path through dry-run modes and automated backup creation.

Highlights:
- Migrates agent personas, memories, skills, and workspace files
- Maps complex OpenClaw model configurations to Hermes YAML format
- Handles credential transfers from local and system-level environment files

---

## Codename-11/hermes-relay

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Codename-11/hermes-relay
GitHub: https://github.com/Codename-11/hermes-relay
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 31
Hermes Companion — Android app for Hermes agent platform. Chat, terminal, and device control over WSS.

Hermes-Relay is a dual-surface companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent from Android and desktop environments. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a server-side plugin to facilitate real-time communication, terminal access, and device control without requiring SSH. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform actions like tapping or swiping, while the desktop CLI and tray app provide local file access, terminal execution, and clipboard integration for the remote agent. This setup allows users to leverage powerful remote compute while maintaining the experience of a locally running agent on their personal devices.

Highlights:
- Native Android app for remote chat, voice, and screen-based device control.
- Desktop CLI and Windows tray app for local-style terminal interaction.
- WSS relay enables agent access to local files and system clipboards.

---

## FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/FahrenheitResearch/hermes-weather-plugin
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 30
NWS-grade model imagery, NEXRAD radar, and verified meteorological calculations. Pure Rust.

The Hermes Weather Plugin provides a suite of 13 meteorological tools for the Hermes Agent ecosystem, enabling real-time access to observations, forecasts, and high-fidelity model imagery. It utilizes a Python-first architecture that offloads heavy computational tasks, such as NEXRAD radar rendering and ECAPE calculations, to high-performance Rust backends. The plugin features an automated bootstrap process that compiles necessary Rust binaries on first use, ensuring a streamlined setup for complex weather analysis. Users can retrieve verified model soundings, severe weather outlooks from the SPC, and global data via Open-Meteo integration.

Highlights:
- Thirteen tools for NWS forecasts, radar imagery, and severe weather alerts
- Rust-backed meteorological calculations for ECAPE, soundings, and parcel profiles
- Automated bootstrap system for compiling and caching required Rust binaries

---

## Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council
GitHub: https://github.com/Ridwannurudeen/hermes-council
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 30
Adversarial multi-perspective council MCP server for decision-making

Hermes Council is an MCP server that provides an adversarial judgment layer for Hermes Agent to stress-test plans and actions before execution. It utilizes a multi-perspective deliberation process involving personas like Advocate, Skeptic, and Arbiter to generate structured verdicts on code diffs, claims, and risky commands. This approach helps prevent overconfident agent failures by producing explicit signals such as blocking risks, required checks, and DPO preference pairs for reinforcement learning workflows.

Highlights:
- Multi-persona adversarial deliberation using Advocate, Skeptic, and Oracle roles
- Structured safety gating with allow, deny, and conditional verdicts
- Generates DPO preference pairs and rewards for RL training workflows

---

## yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/yantrikos/yantrikdb-hermes-plugin
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 30
Self-maintaining Hermes memory plugin with explainable retrieval — every recall comes back with a why_retrieved tag, plus canonicalization, contradiction tracking, and recency ranking. Embedded by default.

yantrikdb-hermes-plugin is a memory provider for Hermes Agent that prevents the loss of important constraints during long sessions. It utilizes a temporal context graph and a maintenance pass to manage memory lifecycles through promotion, demotion, and supersession. The plugin provides explainable recall with scoring reasons and supports DB-native agent-authored skills with an outcome ledger. It is available as a drop-in plugin with an embedded in-process backend by default.

Highlights:
- Explainable recall with why_retrieved tags for every result
- Contradiction tracking via conflicts() and resolve_conflict() primitives
- Embedded in-process backend requiring no separate server or GPU

---

## amanning3390/flowstate-qmd

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/amanning3390/flowstate-qmd
GitHub: https://github.com/amanning3390/flowstate-qmd
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 29
Anticipatory memory with RAG and vector search — Hermes 2026 Hackathon entry

FlowState-QMD is a local-first memory layer designed to provide coding agents with immediate project context from markdown documentation. It utilizes a SQLite-based vector store and an anticipatory cache that prefetches relevant documents by monitoring active agent session logs. This approach allows agents to access design docs, ADRs, and changelogs before initiating a reactive search loop. The system integrates via an MCP server and supports local execution using GGUF models and node-llama-cpp.

Highlights:
- Anticipatory cache prefetches context by watching agent session logs
- Local-first storage using SQLite-vec and GGUF models via node-llama-cpp
- MCP-native server with automated configuration for major coding agent clients

---

## 0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
GitHub: https://github.com/0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 22
Nix package and NixOS module for reproducible Hermes deployment

nix-hermes-agent provides a Nix package and NixOS module for the declarative deployment of Hermes Agent. It allows users to manage the entire agent lifecycle—including configuration, workspace documents, and systemd services—through a single Nix configuration file. The module ensures reproducibility by rendering settings into a structured CLI configuration while keeping sensitive API keys secure via external environment files. It supports both Nix-managed declarative skills and native Hermes CLI interactive skills, providing a stable foundation for production-ready agent infrastructure.

Highlights:
- Declarative management of agent config, documents, and systemd services
- Secure secrets handling via systemd EnvironmentFiles and sops-nix integration
- Hybrid skill support for both Nix-managed and interactive CLI installs

---

## raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare
GitHub: https://github.com/raulvidis/hermes-cloudflare
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 21
Cloudflare Browser Rendering plugin — crawl, scrape, extract content from web pages

This plugin integrates Cloudflare's Browser Rendering API into the Hermes Agent ecosystem to enable web crawling and content extraction from JavaScript-heavy websites. It utilizes Cloudflare's headless browser infrastructure to perform tasks like CSS-selector scraping, PDF generation, and full-page screenshot capture. The tool also features AI-powered structured data extraction using Llama 3.3 70B via Workers AI. Users can automate complex web workflows including sitemap discovery and Markdown conversion directly through agent commands.

Highlights:
- Crawls websites with depth controls and sitemap discovery support
- Extracts structured JSON data using Workers AI and Llama 3.3
- Converts fully rendered JavaScript-heavy pages into clean Markdown

---

## briancaffey/hermes-otel

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/briancaffey/hermes-otel
GitHub: https://github.com/briancaffey/hermes-otel
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 21
OTel Plugin for Hermes Agent

hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin for the Hermes Agent that enables observability by exporting execution data to OTLP-compatible backends. It automatically captures and traces LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as spans to provide visibility into agentic workflows. The plugin supports a wide range of backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, SigNoz, and Jaeger for monitoring traces, metrics, and logs. Users can configure multiple backends simultaneously via a YAML configuration file to fan out telemetry data in parallel.

Highlights:
- Exports LLM tool calls and model invocations as OTel spans
- Supports multiple backends including Phoenix, Langfuse, and SigNoz
- Provides automated token aggregation and session I/O roll-up

---

## xiaonancs/hermes-agent-study

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/xiaonancs/hermes-agent-study
GitHub: https://github.com/xiaonancs/hermes-agent-study
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 20
Chinese deep research series on Hermes Agent internals, OpenClaw lineage, self-evolution, and source-code architecture

This project provides a comprehensive technical analysis and documentation series focused on the internal architecture of the Hermes Agent by Nous Research. It breaks down the system's core components, including its Python-based execution loop, tool registry, and self-evolution engine, while providing nearly 100 architectural diagrams. The research also explores the project's lineage by comparing its structure to OpenClaw and investigating claims regarding its EvoMap-inspired self-improvement logic. Additionally, it tracks rapid version iterations from v0.9.0 to v0.14.0, covering new features like the Transport ABC and autonomous curators.

Highlights:
- Deep source-code analysis of Hermes Agent architecture and evolution engine
- Comparative studies against OpenClaw, EvoMap, and other Agent frameworks
- Detailed documentation of memory systems, skill hubs, and tool registries

---

## carterwayneskhizeine/hermes-agent-windows-R

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/carterwayneskhizeine/hermes-agent-windows-R
GitHub: https://github.com/carterwayneskhizeine/hermes-agent-windows-R
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 19
Windows-native adaptation fork of Hermes Agent with runtime, path, process, and terminal backend compatibility improvements

This project is a Windows-native fork of Hermes Agent designed to resolve compatibility issues with path handling, process management, and terminal encoding. It implements specific adaptations for Git Bash path conversion and introduces a multi-profile runtime system for running parallel instances. The fork includes a dedicated PowerShell-based startup workflow and a web-based Dashboard UI optimized for the Windows environment.

Highlights:
- Fixes Git Bash path handling and terminal encoding for Windows
- Supports multi-profile management and parallel gateway instances
- Includes psmux integration for one-click automated environment launching

---

## JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-monitoring-toolkit

GitHub: https://github.com/JackTheGit/hermes-ai-infrastructure-monitoring-toolkit
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 18
Autonomous monitoring — cron-based research ingestion, cost forecasting, headless systemd

This project is a reference implementation for building autonomous, long-running monitoring systems using Hermes Agent. It utilizes cron-based workflows to ingest AI research from arXiv and track infrastructure repositories on GitHub. The system automatically generates structured markdown reports covering compute risk analysis, cost projections, and trend dashboards. It serves as a template for developers to deploy headless, scheduled agents that transform raw research data into actionable infrastructure intelligence.

Highlights:
- Automated research ingestion via arXiv API and GitHub ecosystem tracking
- Scheduled cron workflows for risk analysis and cost modeling
- Generates terminal-style dashboards and structured markdown intelligence reports

## alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer
GitHub: https://github.com/alias8818/hermes-tool-slimmer
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 17
Reduce Hermes Agent tool-schema overhead with keyword selection and Tool Search support

Hermes Tool Slimmer is a schema-selection optimization that reduces the prompt token overhead caused by large tool catalogs in Hermes Agent. It creates an indexable corpus of tool schemas and uses local BM25 ranking with explicit boosts to select only the most relevant tools for each turn. This process minimizes the repeated payload sent to the model while maintaining essential safety tools. The project includes a dashboard for monitoring estimated token savings and supports custom profiles for different deployment environments like Slack or Telegram.

Highlights:
- Ranks tools using BM25 and keyword selection for efficiency
- Provides a dashboard to track estimated schema token savings
- Supports custom tool profiles and always-include/exclude lists

---

## vectrocomputers/Hermes-Agent-Action-Plan

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/vectrocomputers/Hermes-Agent-Action-Plan
GitHub: https://github.com/vectrocomputers/Hermes-Agent-Action-Plan
Category: Guides & Docs
Stars: 16
Privacy-first practical guidance for configuring and operating Hermes Agent effectively

The Hermes Agent Action Plan is a configuration framework and operational guide designed to optimize Hermes Agent deployments on Ubuntu 24. It provides a structured philosophy for setting up an autonomous assistant that prioritizes local-first tools, persistent memory, and granular system access. By implementing specific security hardening, firewall rules, and communication guidelines, the plan enables the agent to perform complex tasks while maintaining user privacy and auditability. The guide serves as a template for transitioning the agent from a basic chatbot into a capable system administrator or personal assistant.

Highlights:
- Privacy-first configuration prioritizing local and open-source tools
- Structured memory system using daily logs and long-term curated files
- Security-focused system access strategy with audit trails and recovery steps

---

## hxsteric/mercury

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/hxsteric/mercury
GitHub: https://github.com/hxsteric/mercury
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 16
Blockchain cash flow analyzer — multi-chain analysis, fraud detection, WebGL dashboard

Mercury is a blockchain cash flow analyzer and Hermes Agent skill designed to provide transparency into wallet activities across Ethereum, Base, and Solana. It utilizes parallel subagents to fetch transaction data, identify six specific fraud patterns, and generate AI-driven trading strategies based on wallet behavior. The project features a multi-layer WebGL and Canvas dashboard for real-time visualization of inflows, outflows, and risk metrics. It operates as a standalone tool or an integrated Hermes skill for automated multi-chain investigations.

Highlights:
- Analyzes transaction flows across Ethereum, Base, and Solana chains
- Detects six fraud patterns including mixer interactions and dust attacks
- Visualizes data via interactive WebGL and Canvas-based dashboards

---

## Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/Ladybug-Memory/hermes-memory-plugin
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 16
Local-only Hermes memory plugin with built-in 1-10 importance ranking so the things that matter most surface first. Columnar .lbdb embedded graph DB, BM25-first retrieval, optional GLiNER2 entity extraction.

Hermes Ladybug Memory is a local-first memory provider plugin that enables Hermes agents to store and retrieve information without relying on cloud services or external APIs. It utilizes a columnar embedded graph database to manage typed memory entries, named relationships, and importance-weighted recall. The system prioritizes high-value information using a 1-10 importance scale and supports BM25 keyword search for precise retrieval. Users can also enable optional entity extraction via GLiNER2 to build structured knowledge graphs directly on their local machine.

Highlights:
- Local-only storage using a single .lbdb embedded graph database file
- Importance-weighted recall surfaces high-priority memories during context prefetch
- Supports BM25 keyword search and named graph relationships between entries

---

## rodmarkun/anihermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/rodmarkun/anihermes
GitHub: https://github.com/rodmarkun/anihermes
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 15
Local anime server and tracker via natural language for Hermes Agent

AniHermes is a natural language anime media server and tracker built on the Hermes Agent framework. It leverages LLM orchestration to manage downloads via qBittorrent, track series progress, and organize local libraries through conversational interfaces like Telegram, Discord, or CLI. The system utilizes agentic capabilities such as vision analysis for identifying anime from screenshots and automated CRON jobs for scheduling weekly episode downloads. It integrates directly with Anilist for watchlist synchronization and provides parallel search functionality across multiple sources like SubsPlease and Nyaa.

Highlights:
- Natural language control for downloads, tracking, and library management
- Vision-based anime identification from screenshots using Hermes Agent
- Automated weekly episode tracking via NL-defined CRON jobs

---

The wiki contains deployment patterns and practical recipes to assist developers in configuring the system for various environments. By consolidating these resources, the project helps users navigate the setup process and optimize their autonomous email workflows.

## adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud
GitHub: https://github.com/adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 13
Hermes Agent skill for Nextcloud — manage files, notes, calendar, tasks, and contacts via WebDAV and CalDAV.

This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and storing credentials in a restricted local environment file.

Highlights:
- Manages files, notes, calendars, tasks, and contacts via standard protocols.
- Communicates securely over HTTPS using Nextcloud App Passwords.
- Includes a guided setup script for credential validation and configuration.

## 42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/42-evey/evey-bridge-plugin
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 12
Claude Code and Hermes context sharing bridge — auto-checks messages, Mother Mode loop

The Evey Bridge Plugin is a technical integration that connects Claude Code with the Evey hermes-agent stack. It functions by automatically checking for messages or tasks from the bridge and injecting them as context into every prompt submission. The plugin includes specialized commands for running a 'Mother Mode' improvement loop and monitoring system health, including wallet and cron status. It is designed specifically for users running the Evey stack at a designated local directory.

Highlights:
- Injects Evey messages and tasks into Claude Code prompts
- Automates Mother Mode improvement loops via the /evey-loop command
- Monitors health, wallets, and crons with quick status checks

## PederHP/skillsdotnet

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/PederHP/skillsdotnet
GitHub: https://github.com/PederHP/skillsdotnet
Category: Skills & Skill Registries
Stars: 11
C# / .NET implementation of agentskills.io with MCP integration

SkillsDotNet is a C#/.NET implementation of the agentskills.io standard designed to integrate agent skills into the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It uses a skill:// URI convention to implement progressive disclosure, allowing clients to discover lightweight skill metadata before loading full documentation or supporting files. The library provides seamless integration with Microsoft.Extensions.AI and supports automatic discovery of skills from various agent directories like Claude, Cursor, and Copilot.

Highlights:
- Implements progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files
- Integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based skill loading
- Supports automatic registration of common agent skill directories

---

## JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server
GitHub: https://github.com/JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 10
Headless systemd deployment with Nous Portal integration — production-ready

This project provides a deployment framework for running Hermes Agent as a persistent, headless Linux service. It utilizes systemd to manage the Hermes Gateway, ensuring the agent remains active across reboots without requiring an open terminal session. The setup integrates with the Nous Portal for model access and leverages the native Hermes cron scheduler to automate recurring AI tasks. This approach offers a stable, self-hosted alternative to manual shell loops or third-party hosting for autonomous server operations.

Highlights:
- Configures Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service
- Enables autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs
- Supports headless operation on Ubuntu and Debian servers

## jpalmae/hermeshq

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/jpalmae/hermeshq
GitHub: https://github.com/jpalmae/hermeshq
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 9
HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane for running and operating multiple Hermes Agent instances from one web application.

HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to external AI clients like Claude Code and Codex.

Highlights:
- Orchestrates multiple Hermes Agent instances with separate workspaces and identities
- Provides hierarchy-aware inter-agent delegation and task scheduling
- Offers enterprise MCP access for external AI client integrations

---

## nativ3ai/hermes-payguard

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/nativ3ai/hermes-payguard
GitHub: https://github.com/nativ3ai/hermes-payguard
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 8
Safe-by-design USDC and x402 payment plugin for Hermes Agent

Hermes PayGuard is a security-focused plugin that enables Hermes Agents to handle USDC and x402 payments through a gated execution model. It separates payment preparation from execution by requiring an out-of-band human approval stamp for transfers exceeding user-defined policy limits. The system supports Circle developer-controlled wallets, cross-chain CCTP transfers, and automated x402 micropayments for paid HTTP fetches. This architecture ensures that while agents can stage financial intents, they cannot unilaterally move significant funds without explicit operator consent.

Highlights:
- Enforces human-in-the-loop approval for high-value USDC transfers
- Supports Circle CCTP for cross-chain USDC routing and execution
- Automates x402 micropayments below configurable policy thresholds

---

## bryercowan/hermes-embodied

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/bryercowan/hermes-embodied
GitHub: https://github.com/bryercowan/hermes-embodied
Category: Domain Applications
Stars: 8
Self-improving robotics via VLA model fine-tuning on cloud GPUs

Hermes Embodied is a robotics extension for Hermes Agent that enables autonomous fine-tuning of Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models through natural language. The system orchestrates a self-improvement loop by collecting robot trajectories in simulation or hardware, provisioning cloud GPUs via Vast.ai, and retraining models when performance thresholds are met. It integrates with the LeRobot framework and MuJoCo simulation to automate the transition from data collection to model promotion. This allows users to manage complex robotics training pipelines without manual machine learning expertise.

Highlights:
- Automates VLA fine-tuning loops using natural language commands
- Provisions and manages Vast.ai cloud GPU instances autonomously
- Supports SmolVLA and GR00T models for simulation or physical hardware

---

## beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation
GitHub: https://github.com/beardthelion/hermes-skill-distillation
Category: Forks & Derivatives
Stars: 7
Generate agentic training trajectories from real-world tasks for Hermes 4 fine-tuning

This project provides a skill distillation pipeline designed to fine-tune Hermes 4 models using real-world agentic trajectories. It captures tool-use sessions from a diverse 30-task battery and converts them into SFT-ready data or live RL signals for the Atropos training framework. The system uses a multi-dimensional reward function to score trajectories based on task completion, efficiency, and error recovery. This approach enables a closed learning loop where production-style agent interactions serve as the primary training data for future model iterations.

Highlights:
- Generates SFT-ready JSONL data from real-world tool usage trajectories
- Integrates with Atropos for live reinforcement learning via GRPO
- Automated scoring based on completion, efficiency, and error recovery

---

## Xquik-dev/hermes-tweet

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Xquik-dev/hermes-tweet
GitHub: https://github.com/Xquik-dev/hermes-tweet
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 7
Native Hermes Agent plugin for X automation through Xquik

Hermes Tweet is a native plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that enables automated interaction with X (formerly Twitter) via the Xquik API. It integrates 99 OpenAPI-generated endpoints into the agent's environment, allowing for structured social media tasks like searching, posting, and trend monitoring. The plugin utilizes a tiered security model that separates read-only operations from account actions, requiring explicit environment configuration to enable write-access tools. It includes a bundled Hermes skill to provide agents with contextual guidance on how to navigate the available toolset.

Highlights:
- Provides 99 agent-callable Xquik endpoints for comprehensive X automation
- Separates read and action tools for least-privilege security enforcement
- Includes slash commands for quick account status and trend checks

---

## Context4GPTs/klodi-plugin

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/Context4GPTs/klodi-plugin
GitHub: https://github.com/Context4GPTs/klodi-plugin
Category: Plugins & Extensions
Stars: 7
The agent-to-agent marketplace, in your agent host. Your agent lists, haggles, and closes deals for you while you live your life.

Klodi is an agent-to-agent marketplace plugin that enables AI agents to list, negotiate, and purchase items on behalf of their users. The system functions through a peer-to-peer architecture where agents interact via structured offers and private negotiation channels while keeping floor prices and strategies stored locally. This approach allows users to delegate the entire lifecycle of a transaction—from initial listing to final sign-off—without exposing private bargaining limits to the marketplace. It provides cross-platform compatibility with adapters for Hermes, OpenClaw, and various Rust-based agent hosts.

Highlights:
- Automated agent-led negotiation for buying and selling items
- Local policy files keep floor prices and strategies private
- Cross-platform adapters for Hermes, OpenClaw, and Rust hosts

---

## MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory
GitHub: https://github.com/MukundaKatta/hermes-agentmemory
Category: Memory & Context
Stars: 6
Pull-model episodic memory plugin for Hermes Agent with real deletion (no persistent summaries that haunt future recall) and a trace.jsonl audit log of every memory operation. Community fix for issue #6715. MIT.

hermes-agentmemory is a standalone memory plugin for Hermes Agent that ensures episodic memory deletions are complete and immediate. It utilizes a pull-model approach with synchronous writes and on-demand summarization via Claude models, avoiding the persistent derived summaries found in background-consolidation backends. The plugin provides a transparent audit trail of all memory injections through a JSONL trace log. It is designed for users who require reversible growth and auditable memory operations.

Highlights:
- Immediate, complete deletion of episodic memories without derived artifacts
- Detailed audit logs of every prefetch in trace.jsonl
- On-demand summarization using the Claude model family

---

## ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes
GitHub: https://github.com/ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes
Category: Deployment & Infra
Stars: 5
Docker Compose stack with Portainer UI and ttyd web terminal

This project provides a Docker Compose configuration for deploying the Hermes agent alongside a ttyd web terminal. It utilizes a pre-built image from the GitHub Container Registry to facilitate browser-based terminal access to the agent. Users can manage the deployment as a Git-based stack within the Portainer UI for simplified container orchestration. The setup includes an automated GitHub Action workflow for building and updating the underlying container image.

Highlights:
- Deploys Hermes agent with a ttyd web terminal interface
- Integrates directly with Portainer as a Git Repository stack
- Automates image builds using GitHub Actions and GHCR

---

## gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle
GitHub: https://github.com/gizdusum/hermes-blockchain-oracle
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 4
Solana blockchain intelligence MCP server — wallets, whales, tokens, NFTs, network health

Hermes Blockchain Oracle is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides Hermes Agent with real-time access to the Solana blockchain. It functions as a bridge between natural language queries and on-chain data by exposing specialized tools for wallet, token, and transaction analysis. The server enables users to monitor network health, track whale movements, and manage NFT portfolios through conversational interactions. By integrating directly with Solana RPC nodes, it eliminates the need for manual block explorer searches.

Highlights:
- Provides real-time Solana wallet balances and token portfolio tracking
- Detects large-scale whale transfers with configurable movement thresholds
- Retrieves NFT metadata, collection details, and floor price information

---

## kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc
GitHub: https://github.com/kevinmarmstrong/hermes-claude-code-rc
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 2
Hermes Agent plugin for remote controlling Claude Code sessions via Telegram

This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and direct shell access with built-in safety filters for dangerous operations.

Highlights:
- Starts Claude Code remote control sessions via Telegram slash commands
- Provides mobile access to local filesystems and MCP servers
- Executes headless commands and shell operations on the host machine

---

## BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort
GitHub: https://github.com/BORT-AGENTS/hermes-bort
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 2
No description

hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT (BAP-578) agent NFTs on the BSC mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read on-chain agent states, execute actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory anchored to IPFS. The plugin integrates with the hermes-agent-self-evolution optimizer to commit verifiable on-chain learning records and evolved skills directly to an agent's knowledge registry.

Highlights:
- Reads full agent state, IPFS identity, and knowledge registries
- Executes on-chain actions via VaultPermissionManagerV2 with local policy gating
- Anchors local session memory to IPFS and KnowledgeRegistryV2

---

## MorvaniLabs/cordn8

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/MorvaniLabs/cordn8
GitHub: https://github.com/MorvaniLabs/cordn8
Category: Multi-Agent & Orchestration
Stars: 1
Local-first coordination layer for multiple AI agents using an append-only markdown channel, Hermes adapter, approval modes, and audit trail

Cordn8 is a local-first coordination layer designed to manage multiple AI agents within a single repository. It functions by maintaining an append-only markdown channel that serves as a git-visible audit trail for every agent action and decision. The system supports three operational modes—Manual, Copilot, and Autopilot—to control agent autonomy and includes the Maya release gate for catching regressions. It features a universal adapter surface that integrates various models including Hermes, GPT, Claude, and local Llama instances.

Highlights:
- Append-only markdown channel for auditable, local-first agent coordination
- Three autonomy modes ranging from manual approval to autopilot execution
- Universal adapters for Hermes, NVIDIA NIM, OpenAI, and Anthropic models

---

## kfa-ai/hermes-timetree-sync

URL: https://hermesatlas.com/projects/kfa-ai/hermes-timetree-sync
GitHub: https://github.com/kfa-ai/hermes-timetree-sync
Category: Integrations & Bridges
Stars: 1
Non-interactive TimeTree calendar bridge for Hermes Agent

hermes-timetree-sync is a Python-based CLI and library that enables Hermes Agents to manage TimeTree calendar events without manual user intervention. Since TimeTree discontinued its official API, this bridge uses authenticated web endpoints and session cookies to perform non-interactive writes. It supports creating all-day events directly via HTTP requests and timed events through a Safari-backed automation path. The tool allows agents to translate natural language requests into structured calendar updates using locally stored credentials and optional label mapping policies.

Highlights:
- Syncs all-day and timed events using TimeTree web endpoints
- Supports non-interactive batch writes for multiple calendar entries
- Maps natural language terms to calendar labels via YAML policy
